
## 2026-05-18 - D227/OPS17 deployment/operability readiness verification

Assessment: Deployment files changed repeatedly for worker runtime, export storage, PDF renderer, browser packaging, smoke scripts, and notifications. Before UX, the deployment package needed a fresh config/package/build gate.

Task: Rendered local and VPS Docker Compose configs from checked-in env examples. Ran focused deployment package tests, Node deployment-package tests, and build. No deployment patch was needed.

Verification: Local compose config passed. VPS compose config passed. Focused `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 16/16. `node --test tests/deployment-package/*.test.mjs` passed 24/24. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Remaining risk: This did not run a live full-stack staging smoke. Next assessment starts at D228 report/export product completeness.

## 2026-05-18 - D226/NOTIF07 withdrawal request operational notification foundation

Assessment: Report PDF terminal states already had operational notifications, but withdrawal request creation did not. After QA19 confirmed withdrawal backend trust, the notification gap was operational visibility for newly created withdrawal requests, especially public anonymous-token requests.

Task: Added `withdrawal_request_created` operational notification constants/factory and same-transaction notification insertion for newly created withdrawal requests. Added Docker-backed runtime coverage to prove anonymous token consumption creates a safe notification payload with no raw token leakage. Idempotent existing-request returns remain non-duplicating.

Verification: RED anonymous-token consume test failed at compile on missing notification constants. GREEN focused test passed 1/1. Docker-backed `WithdrawalRuntimeStoreTests|OperationalNotificationStoreTests` passed 79/79. Operational notification API filter passed 6 with 1 Docker-gated skip. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Remaining risk: This is backend/in-app notification visibility only. No email sending, requester acknowledgement, notification UI, or live smoke for withdrawal notifications was added. Next assessment starts at D227 deployment/operability readiness.

## 2026-05-18 - D225/QA19 withdrawal finalization regression sweep

Assessment: Withdrawal backend trust is already implemented through RET20 and safety-swept through QA09, so D225 should verify current evidence instead of inventing another withdrawal feature slice.

Task: Ran a fresh endpoint/model/RLS/Docker-backed runtime regression over withdrawal and related model tests. Investigated the only failure with root-cause debugging.

Root cause/fix: `ApplicationDbContextModelTests.Export_artifact_model_maps_to_expected_table_indexes_constraints_and_foreign_keys` had a stale assertion that every external-object storage row requires `storage_key IS NOT NULL`. The model intentionally allows non-succeeded external attempts without storage keys and requires keys for succeeded external artifacts in `ck_export_artifact_materialization_shape`. Updated the storage-shape assertion to require `content IS NULL` instead.

Verification: Initial regression failed 1/124. Focused failed model test passed 1/1 after the assertion fix. Final withdrawal/model/RLS/endpoint regression passed 124/124 with `RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1`. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Remaining risk: Withdrawal productization now belongs to notifications/admin UX/legal/live validation rather than another anonymous-token backend foundation. Next assessment starts at D226.

## 2026-05-18 - D224/PDF07 product-spine signed download URL smoke coverage

Assessment: Browser runtime and direct PDF delivery checks existed, but owner-runnable product-spine smoke did not verify signed-download-url behavior or safe local-storage unsupported semantics.

Task: Added a static deployment package test and updated `deploy/staging/smoke-product-spine.ps1` with `Assert-ReportPdfArtifactSignedDownloadUrlState`. Succeeded report PDF artifacts now check `/export-artifacts/{id}/signed-download-url`, accept either real signed URL success or `export_artifact_object.signed_urls_not_supported`, and assert no `storageKey` or secret leakage.

Verification: RED focused `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` failed 1/16 for missing signed URL smoke. GREEN focused tests passed 16/16. `smoke-product-spine.ps1` parsed with no errors. `node --test tests/deployment-package/*.test.mjs` passed 24/24. Signed-download-url endpoint tests passed 3/3. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Remaining risk: Live full-stack smoke against running local/S3 staging was not executed. Next assessment starts at D225 withdrawal finalization.

## 2026-05-18 - D223/QA18 post-OPS16 runtime regression

Assessment: OPS16 statically packaged Chromium and renderer defaults, but product confidence still required real image build and runtime browser proof.

Task: Verified Docker tooling, built staging API and worker images with the staging env file, then invoked `/usr/bin/chromium` headlessly inside both built images.

Verification: `docker --version` passed with Docker 29.3.1. `docker compose version` passed with Compose v5.1.1. `docker compose --env-file deploy/staging/env.example -f deploy/staging/docker-compose.yml build api worker` passed. API and worker `docker run --entrypoint /usr/bin/chromium ... --dump-dom` checks passed and rendered `pdf-runtime-ok` with exit code 0. Chromium emitted DBus stderr noise, but it did not block rendering or command success.

Remaining risk: Product-level report PDF endpoint rendering and signed/local artifact delivery are still not proven. Next assessment starts at D224.

## 2026-05-18 - D222/OPS16 staging browser runtime packaging

Assessment: OPS15 exposed report PDF renderer configuration, but staging API/worker images did not package a browser runtime. That left container report PDF generation dependent on custom image/host paths or safe failed-artifact behavior.

Task: Added/used static deployment tests for Chromium packaging and `/usr/bin/chromium` renderer defaults. Updated API and worker Dockerfiles to install Chromium and `fonts-dejavu-core`, aligned compose/env defaults, and updated the VPS staging runbook.

Verification: RED focused `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` failed 2/15 for missing Chromium/default path. GREEN focused tests passed 15/15. `node --test tests/deployment-package/*.test.mjs` passed 24/24. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Remaining risk: Static tests prove package declarations and build compilation, not live Docker image build success or real PDF rendering in the VPS runtime. Next assessment should start from D223.
# Session Log

Append-only. Each session writes one entry. Never edit prior entries; corrections are new entries.

Format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD — <agent name / human handle>
**Goal:** what you set out to do
**Did:** files created / edited (with paths)
**Decisions:** ADRs proposed/accepted (numbers)
**Open questions raised:** with link to OPEN-QUESTIONS entry
**Next:** what the next session should pick up
```

---

## 2026-05-04 — claude-opus (with owner)

**Goal:** Walk through ADRs 0001-0008 with owner, mark Accepted/Rejected per case, apply load-bearing tightenings discovered during review. Close owner-only blockers O04, O05, O06.

**Did:**

- Walked through and **Accepted all 8 ADRs** in single session, applying tightenings at acceptance:
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0001-postgres-over-sqlserver.md` → Accepted; pgcrypto + RLS test mandate promoted to load-bearing; raised Q-048 (RLS cross-tenant test pattern)
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0002-vertical-slices-cqrs-lite.md` → Accepted; MediatR chosen (small-business tier free); pipeline order locked; hand-rolled `Result<T,Error>`; pure-engine no-DI rule
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0003-instrument-library-aggregate.md` → Accepted; scope broadened to non-psychometric instruments via `domain` enum (psychometric / ergonomic / medical / educational / regulatory / other); two-step canonical seeding; derivative aggregate boundary (`parent_instrument_id`, `is_global=false`, `validity_status='derived'`); CHECK-constrained translation table; explicit `is_global` RLS bypass; license lifecycle (`license_expires_at`)
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0004-frontend-stack-split.md` → Accepted; tier strategy locked (Essential free for M1, defer Basic to M2 reassessment, skip PRO/Enterprise); **no SurveyJS Creator in M1** — custom derivative editor instead; PuppeteerSharp for PDF; CI bundle budgets; offline-sync conflict rules; PWA SW versioning + update prompt
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0005-anonymous-longitudinal-self-code.md` → Accepted with **full cryptographic rewrite**: SHA-256 was structurally enumerable (~0.5 ms for 19-bit recipes on a single GPU); replaced with Argon2id (`m=64MiB, t=3, p=4`) + per-campaign-series salt + ≥60-bit recipe entropy floor enforced at recipe creation + security-question fallback forbidden + federated salt model planned for M5 + raw-code log-scrubbing middleware required + single-factor-withdrawal documented as design tradeoff
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0006-fork-vs-refactor.md` → Accepted; repo working name `instruments-platform`; license proprietary closed-source with AGPL/SSPL dependency ban; v1 stays demoable through M2/first-customer; v1 reference-review practice required when implementing v2 features with v1 equivalents
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0007-validated-instrument-lifecycle-m1.md` → Accepted with cross-references to ADRs 0001-0006 + 0008 (each one binding M1 implementation); Q-047 named as platform-canonical OLBI launch blocker; "do not split M1" guard added
  - `docs/v2/70-decisions/0008-scoring-rule-json-source-of-truth.md` → Accepted with 7 implementation tightenings — schema versioning + forward-compatible operation registry, engine snapshotting per campaign launch, determinism guarantee, visual builder = subset of operation palette invariant, per-instrument fixtures mandatory, validation UX as load-bearing requirement, Q-043 promoted to critical path
- Updated `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md` — closed 9 questions, raised 1
- Updated `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/NEXT-ACTIONS.md` — moved O04, O05, O06 to Done; added A36-A49 (14 new agent actions)

**Decisions:** ADR-0001 through ADR-0008 all Accepted (status: Accepted). Owner-only blockers O04 (review ADRs), O05 (repo name → `instruments-platform`), O06 (license → proprietary closed-source) all closed.

**Methodology note:** This was an owner-driven walkthrough session, not an agent-execution session. Each ADR was presented with my recommendation, doubts, doubts-with-evidence (especially the ADR-0005 cryptographic flaw), and explicit "Accept / push back" decision points. Owner accepted with one verbal pushback worth recording: timeline estimates for M1 dropped from acceptance notes per owner's swarm-execution strategy ("calendar shrinks, work content unchanged, owner attention is the new bottleneck"). The cryptographic correction in ADR-0005 was load-bearing and would have shipped a structurally false anonymity claim if the ADR had been rubber-stamped.

## 2026-05-04 — claude-opus (with owner) — A48 dispatch

**Goal:** Resolve Q-043 — produce the final 🟡 Proposed shape of the scoring-rule JSON schema per ADR-0008. Land all 9 required sections in `10-domain/scoring-rules-spec.md`. Close Q-043; partial-close Q-045 if mixed-version compatibility work supports it. Move A48 to Done.

**Did:**

- Rewrote `docs/v2/10-domain/scoring-rules-spec.md` end-to-end to 🟡 Proposed final, all 9 sections covered:
  1. Operation palette — 15 M1 ops with typed inputs/outputs and JSON encoding (`select_answers`, `reverse_code`, `mean`, `sum`, `weighted_mean`, `weighted_sum`, `subscale_aggregate`, `normalize_0_100`, `combine`, `difference`, `percentile_vs_norm`, `bucket`, `threshold`, `if_else`, `count_valid`); explicit extensibility hooks for COPSOQ-III subscale composition, UWES-9 mean-with-reverse, MBI subscale + cut-points; closed type system; per-op JSON-Schema fragment example.
  2. `schema_version` semver + forward-compatible operation registry (`engine/registry/v1.json`); minor/major/patch rules; version-negotiation algorithm; soft-deprecation policy (≥12 months + ≥2 minor versions retained).
  3. Validation error envelope JSON schema (`code`, `severity`, `message`, RFC 6901 `pointer`, `line`/`column`, `op`, `node_id`, `expected`/`actual`, `did_you_mean` suggestions, `doc_link`); UX requirements as load-bearing — live validation, runnable preview, plain-language messages, copy-error-to-clipboard, no silent partial save.
  4. Fixture format chosen as **YAML** (with rationale); `fixtures/instruments/<code>/v<version>/canonical/*.yaml` location convention; per-instrument suite minimum (happy-path, missing-data, all-reverse-coded, norm-overlay); CI gate with CODEOWNERS.
  5. Mixed-version wave compatibility (partial-closes Q-045): three-state model — `compatible` / `descriptive_only` / `incompatible` — with default-deny for undeclared cross-version deltas; per-output `scope` overrides; resolved at report time against snapshot.
  6. Engine snapshotting: snapshot row + frozen-document JSONB hybrid; `wave.scoring_engine_version` + `scoring_rule_document_hash` + frozen `rule_document`; engine retirement policy (never within major; ≥24 months + migration before drop in v2 major).
  7. Determinism guarantee — explicit allow/forbid lists (clock, env, RNG without seed, IO all forbidden); deterministic input sorting + Kahan summation; six-test cadence (fixture-replay, cross-engine, cross-platform, float-stability lint, Kahan unit, snapshot-replay).
  8. Visual builder = strict subset invariant — per-op builder/expert classification table; banner + read-only graph behavior when expert-only ops present; round-trip byte-identical invariant; promotion process via ADR amendment.
  9. Full worked OLBI canonical scoring rule JSON (EX/DI subscales + OVERALL, per published reverse-coding); paired derivative example (60/40 EX/DI weighting) referencing canonical via `score_ref`.
- Updated `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`:
  - Closed Q-043 with one-paragraph resolution + spec link.
  - Partial-closed Q-045 (three-state model documented; full close pending owner sign-off + first researcher walkthrough of mixed-version report wording).
- Updated `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/NEXT-ACTIONS.md`:
  - Removed A48 from open queue.
  - Added A48 (Done, 2026-05-04) entry under Done section.
  - Updated A49 entry (now unblocks: A48 dependency removed; only Q-047 remains).

**Decisions:** No new ADRs. The spec implements ADR-0008 strategy without expanding scope. Scoping decisions made within ADR-0008 latitude:

- YAML chosen for fixture format (over JSON-only) for human-author ergonomics; engine normalizes to canonical JSON at load.
- Snapshot persistence chosen as hybrid (normalized columns + frozen JSONB) over either pure-normalized or pure-JSONB; informs Q-042 schema work without pre-empting it.
- Default for cross-version deltas is **deny** (must declare compatibility) rather than warn-and-allow — matches existing `difference` op behavior already in the spec and matches `research-exports.md` block-or-descriptive policy.
- Operation palette set at 15 ops (M1 viable for OLBI + extensibility hooks named for COPSOQ-III/UWES-9/MBI) per ADR-0008 directive to avoid scope-creep to "every conceivable instrument."

**Open questions raised:** None new.

**Open questions touched:**

- **Q-043 closed.** Resolution links to scoring-rules-spec.md; A48 done.
- **Q-045 partial-closed.** Three-state compatibility metadata model defined; full close pending owner confirmation that `descriptive_only` is a useful distinct state vs collapsing into `incompatible` and pending researcher walkthrough of mixed-version report wording.
- **Q-042** (campaign launch snapshot physical model) — not closed; scoring-rules-spec.md Section 6 declares which scoring fields must be in the snapshot; the snapshot table schema work itself is still under Q-042.
- **Q-047** (OLBI canonical source + license) — untouched; remains platform-canonical OLBI launch blocker. The worked-example fixture in Section 4 is illustrative; it does not become canonical-published until Q-047 resolves and A49 sources real OLBI items + scoring with license evidence.

**Methodology note:** Single-pass agent execution against the dispatch prompt's 9-section requirement. The prompt's required reading list (ADR-0008, ADR-0007, ADR-0003, scoring-rules-spec.md current state, instruments-catalog.md, instrument-library.md, research-exports.md) was sufficient — no additional doc lookup needed for spec design itself, only for cross-link verification (campaign-lifecycle.md, dashboards-and-reports.md). The catalog's OLBI metadata (`reverse_coded_items: [3, 6, 9, 12, 15]`, `scale: 4-point Likert (1=Strongly agree, 4=Strongly disagree)`) was load-bearing for the worked example direction.

**Next:**

1. Owner reviews scoring-rules-spec.md, especially three-state mixed-version compatibility (Section 5) — confirm `descriptive_only` is a useful distinct state or collapse to `incompatible` + warning. If owner confirms, fully close Q-045.
2. Owner / next agent: progress Q-047 (OLBI license + source evidence). Once Q-047 resolves, A49 (per-instrument scoring fixture sourcing for OLBI) becomes runnable and the worked-example fixture can be replaced with the real canonical fixture.
3. Subsequent agent priorities (unchanged): A38 (apply ADR-0003 schema edits), A45 (apply ADR-0005 crypto spec), A19 (draft ADR-0009 outbox + audit), A20 (draft ADR-0010 RLS-enforced tenancy), A36 (RLS cross-tenant test pattern).

- `dotnet ef migrations has-pending-model-changes --project src/Platform.Infrastructure/Platform.Infrastructure.csproj --startup-project src/Platform.Infrastructure/Platform.Infrastructure.csproj --no-build` - no pending model changes.

## 2026-05-06 - codex - F06 audit foundation

**Goal:** Add the in-transaction audit foundation without letting retention or partition automation be assumed complete.

**Did:**

- Added `Platform.Domain.Auditing` with `AuditEvent`, actor/change constants, and JSON helper.
- Added `ICurrentAuditContext` / `CurrentAuditContext` in Application.
- Added `AuditSaveChangesInterceptor` in Infrastructure and registered it with `ApplicationDbContext`.
- Added EF mapping and migration `AddAuditEvents`, using raw PostgreSQL DDL for a partitioned `audit_event` parent table, default partition, tenant RLS policy, indexes, and append-only trigger.
- Added Docker-backed tests for audit write/diff behavior, rollback atomicity, tenant RLS reads, and update/delete rejection.
- Updated backlog/handoff/schema docs to state F06 does **not** complete retention rules or automated monthly partition creation/drop; those remain Q-050/A14/deployment-runbook work.

**Decisions:** F06 creates a default audit partition so inserts work immediately; monthly partition automation is intentionally deferred until retention and deployment docs are specified.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-050 remains open and now explicitly tracks audit retention plus partition lifecycle automation.

**Next:** Continue with F07 outbox interceptor/relay, F08 OIDC, F09 subject model, A37 slice generator, or owner blockers.

## 2026-05-06 - codex - F07 outbox foundation

**Goal:** Add the transactional outbox foundation paired with F06 audit, while keeping scheduler and payload-policy gaps visible.

- Added `Platform.Domain.Outbox` with `OutboxEvent`, `OutboxMessage`, `OutboxPayload`, retry/dead-letter mechanics, and bounded `last_error` storage.
- Added scoped `IOutboxEventBuffer` / `OutboxEventBuffer` and `OutboxSaveChangesInterceptor` so handlers can enqueue domain events and persist them atomically on `SaveChanges`.
- Added `outbox_event` EF mapping and migration `AddOutboxEvents` with relay indexes and tenant RLS policy.
- Added `Platform.Workers.Outbox.OutboxRelay` and `IOutboxEventDispatcher`; relay claims due rows with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, dispatches, marks published, or schedules retry/dead-letter.
- Added Docker-backed tests for outbox atomic writes, rollback behavior, relay success, and relay failure retry.
- Updated docs/backlog/open questions so Hangfire scheduling/bootstrap remains Q-049/deployment wiring and payload cap/pointer strategy remains Q-051.

**Decisions:** F07 implements the relay unit but does not wire Hangfire scheduling yet; that remains a deployment/bootstrap decision under Q-049.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-049 and Q-051 remain open by design.

**Next:** Continue with F08 OIDC, F09 subject model, F10 instrument metadata model, A37 slice generator, or owner blockers.

**Goal:** Add the subject graph foundation for audiences/respondent rules while closing the subject-vs-user ambiguity.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A25 threshold alerts spec

**Goal:** Replace the threshold-alerts stub with a feature spec that future implementation can follow without breaking anonymity or overstating clinical safety behavior.

**Did:**

- Populated `30-features/threshold-alerts.md` with aggregate-first threshold rules over versioned score outputs and tightly controlled direct-item safety triggers.
- Defined identity-mode routing limits: respondent messages can work across modes, aggregate notifications can work with disclosure/k-anonymity controls, and individual wellbeing/EAP routing is limited to identified flows with consent, protocol, permission, and audit.
- Added target data-model deltas for threshold rules and alert events, including the need for nullable `alert_event.response_session_id` for aggregate-only alerts.
- Added rule publish gates, alert state machine, respondent-message requirements, audit/compliance controls, observability metrics, and test gates.
- Updated handoff files so A25 is done and A27 is next in the agent-executable queue.

**Decisions:** The platform is not a clinical decision engine or emergency dispatcher. Anonymous and anonymous-longitudinal alerts must not expose an individual response, participant code, trajectory identifier, or inferable operator workflow.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-013 remains open and blocks PHQ-9 or similar suicide/self-harm risk production rules until a qualified safety protocol, disclosure, resource catalog, and identified/anonymous behavior are approved.

**Not done:** No backend alert-rule table/migration, alert evaluator, notification delivery, alert inbox UI, support-protocol model, PHQ-9 shipping, or emergency workflow implementation.

**Next:** A27 designer UI is now top of the agent-executable queue. A31 pricing and A30 sales narratives remain useful doc follow-ups.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A28 federated campaigns spec

**Goal:** Replace the federated-campaigns stub with an M5 feature spec for multi-PI/cross-institution studies without weakening tenant isolation.

**Did:**

- Populated `30-features/federated-campaigns.md` with purpose, M5 scope, non-goals, concepts, federation/collaborator lifecycle, setup flow, roles, target data-model deltas, legal/compliance gates, identity-mode behavior, aggregation/reporting/export rules, RLS/security rules, UI surfaces, implementation phases, tests, and local open questions.
- Made the default sharing model explicit: each tenant keeps raw data tenant-local; federation composes approved aggregate projections.
- Defined federation-level anonymous-longitudinal salt behavior for M5 cross-site trajectory linkage, including secret handling, audit, no export/log exposure, and rotation-by-new-series semantics.
- Aligned the legal section with the current GDPR compliance posture: legal role model is reviewed and recorded, not auto-decided by the product.
- Updated handoff files so A28 is done and A29 risks is next in the agent-executable queue.

**Decisions:** Federation is an explicit audited system workflow, not a general multi-tenant query mode. Coordinator tenants can manage protocol/readiness and approved outputs, but are not superusers over collaborator tenants. Aggregate-only is the default; pseudonymous/identified/raw row-level exports are exceptional and require explicit legal, ethics, site, export-scope, and audit approval.

**Open questions raised:** None global. M5-local questions remain inside the spec: exact legal templates, federation salt secret storage, site-labeled comparison default, whether pseudonymous row-level export is worth implementing, site withdrawal after external publication, and non-EEA consortium posture.

**Not done:** No federation domain implementation, migrations, invitation workflow, aggregate projection worker, composition job, report/export UI, federation-level salt secret workflow, or row-level export approval workflow.

**Next:** A29 risks is now top of the agent-executable queue. A31 pricing and A30 sales narratives remain useful follow-ups.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A29 roadmap risks register

**Goal:** Replace the risks stub with an operating risk register that reflects the current roadmap, blockers, compliance posture, and business assumptions.

**Did:**

- Populated `60-roadmap/risks.md` with rating model, risk appetite, top risks, market/technical/regulatory/financial/key-person registers, owners, early warning signals, mitigations, contingencies, blocked-work map, review cadence, source index, and related links.
- Folded in current project blockers and guardrails: O01/O02/O03 buyer validation, Q-047 OLBI platform-canonical rights, Q-053 production GDPR package, D17 thin setup/UI proof path, A16/A36/A45 security/anonymity gates, and A28 M5 federation posture.

- Updated handoff files so A29 is done and A30 sales narratives is next in the agent-executable queue.

**Decisions:** Treat the register as an operating gate, not background context. Zero-tolerance items include cross-tenant leakage, raw participant-code/salt/token leakage, unsafe production compliance claims, rights-unclear platform instrument claims, and safety-sensitive clinical alerting without reviewed protocol.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Existing Q-004, Q-005, Q-013, Q-047, Q-053, Q-020, Q-021, Q-028, Q-049, Q-050, and Q-051 remain the main active risk drivers.

**Not done:** No risk automation, no dashboard, no final legal review, no customer validation calls, no pricing/sales narrative changes, and no mitigation implementation beyond documentation.

**Next:** A30 sales narratives is now top of the agent-executable queue. A31 pricing is also unblocked and useful soon.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A30 sales narratives

**Goal:** Replace the sales-narratives stub with practical discovery, pitch, demo, objection, pricing-posture, and close material for target buyers P1-P7.

- Added immediate outreach scripts for O01 prof lead, O02 secondary academic contacts, and O03 OSH consultants.
- Added pipeline stages and metrics to track call outcomes.
- Updated handoff files so A30 is done and A31 pricing is next in the agent-executable queue.

**Decisions:** Keep the sales narrative narrow: validated wellbeing/research/OSH measurement, not a generic survey builder or engagement suite. Treat the current proof as setup-first, not a finished M1 product. Keep OLBI and production compliance claims behind Q-047 and Q-053 respectively.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-001, Q-004, Q-005, Q-006, Q-047, Q-053, and Q-013 remain key sales/positioning blockers.

**Not done:** No public marketing copy, no final pricing, no outbound emails sent, no customer validation calls completed, no reference customers, and no production sales collateral.

**Next:** A31 pricing is now top of the agent-executable queue.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A31 pricing

Keep direct high-touch tenants, hospital/public-sector deals, self-host/single-tenant, custom legal/security work, translations, reports, and paid instruments outside cheap monthly pricing.

Existing Q-047, Q-053, Q-010, Q-033, Q-002, Q-020, and Q-021 still matter.

**Not done:** No public pricing page, no Stripe/billing implementation, no quote template, no customer calls, no final legal/compliance terms, no vendor/reseller terms for paid instruments.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A34 observability

**Goal:** Replace the observability stub with a telemetry, dashboard, SLO, alerting, and logging/privacy contract for future implementation.

**Did:**

- Populated `40-ops/observability.md` with current repo gaps, purpose, OpenTelemetry/OTLP stack direction, resource attributes, correlation rules, structured logging rules, sensitive-data exclusions, log retention, tracing, metrics, health checks, initial SLOs, dashboard set, alert classes, frontend telemetry limits, release observability, operational-vs-audit log boundary, implementation backlog, and production gates.
- Kept the spec documentation-only: no OpenTelemetry packages, collector/Alloy config, Docker Compose, dashboards, or alert rules were added.
- Source-checked official docs for OpenTelemetry .NET/OTLP/Collector/HTTP conventions, Microsoft .NET observability and ASP.NET Core health checks, Npgsql diagnostics/tracing/metrics, Grafana/Alloy/Tempo/Loki/SLOs, Prometheus, and OWASP logging guidance.
- Updated handoff files so A34 is done and A35 runbook is now the next agent-executable queue item. Also cleared stale A33 blockers from A35 and A44 because deployment is now populated.

**Decisions:** OpenTelemetry is the platform telemetry contract. Preferred production path is API/workers emitting OTLP to OpenTelemetry Collector or Grafana Alloy, then traces to Tempo, metrics to Prometheus or Grafana Cloud/Mimir-compatible storage, logs to Loki, and dashboards/alerts in Grafana. Audit logs remain separate from operational telemetry.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-020, Q-021, Q-049, Q-050, Q-051, and Q-053 still affect production observability, especially hosted-vs-self-hosted choice, worker metrics, audit retention, outbox payload handling, and log retention/legal posture.

**Not done:** No runtime instrumentation, health endpoint implementation changes, redaction registry code, collector/Alloy deployment, Grafana dashboards, alert rules, SLO recording rules, frontend beacons, or runbook procedures.

**Next:** A35 runbook is now top of the agent-executable queue. It should use A33 deployment and A34 observability as the alert/procedure backbone.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A35 runbook

**Goal:** Replace the runbook placeholder with practical incident procedures grounded in the deployment, observability, security, backup/DR, and compliance docs.

**Did:**

- Populated `40-ops/runbook.md` with current implementation limits, operating rules, severity levels, incident-command roles, lifecycle, triage checklist, communication/status templates, rollback/forward-fix rules, restore drill procedure, break-glass controls, escalation matrix, post-incident review template, on-call rotation, production readiness gates, implementation backlog, and source index.
- Added response procedures for database/pool exhaustion, failed deployment, migration/post-deploy SQL failure, outbox backlog/dead letters, stuck worker/scoring/export jobs, email/webhook failures, frontend/runtime asset failures, future Redis outage, object-storage/export artifact failure, RLS/tenant leak, PII/token/raw-code/log leak, token brute-force, auth/OIDC outage, SLO burn, backup/restore failure, and dependency/security scan criticals.
- Corrected the old SurveyJS CDN assumption: respondent runtime should be bundled from platform origin; a runtime SurveyJS CDN dependency is treated as a deployment/security regression.
- Kept the spec documentation-only and explicit that production Dockerfiles/workflows/scripts, observability implementation, backup automation, status page, and legal/compliance gates are not done.
- Updated handoff files so A35 is done and A37 slice scaffolding generator is now the next agent-executable queue item.

**Decisions:** Use incident-command structure even in solo mode. Treat RLS failures, tenant leaks, sensitive log leaks, raw-code/token/answer exposure, destructive migrations, and confirmed data corruption as SEV0 by default. App rollback and database rollback stay separate: app/static artifacts can roll back; database state normally forward-fixes, with restore only after explicit owner approval of RPO/data-loss impact.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-020, Q-021, Q-049, Q-050, Q-051, and Q-053 remain production-affecting blockers.

**Not done:** No deployment automation, health endpoint changes, alert rules, dashboards, backup scripts, restore automation, status page tooling, break-glass implementation, dead-letter replay tooling, token rotation tooling, or production launch.

**Next:** A37 slice scaffolding generator is now top of the agent-executable queue.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A37 slice scaffolding generator

**Goal:** Add a repo-local generator so new backend work follows ADR-0002 vertical-slice shape by default.

**Did:**

- Added `templates/platform-slice` as a `dotnet new platform-slice` item template.
- Template generates `src/Platform.Application/Features/<Feature>/<Slice>/` request, handler, validator, endpoint, and response files.
- Template generates `tests/Platform.UnitTests/Application/<Feature>/<Slice>/` slice registration tests.
- Added `--feature`, `--requestKind Command|Query`, and `--httpVerb Get|Post|Put|Delete` parameters.
- Generated handlers return a visible `NotImplemented` `Result<T>` failure so scaffolds compile and generated tests pass until real behavior replaces the placeholder.
- Added xUnit tests that install and instantiate the template through the real `dotnet new` flow in an isolated temporary `DOTNET_CLI_HOME`.
- Added backend-stack usage docs.
- Updated handoff files so A37 is done and A39 is the next agent-executable queue item.

**Decisions:** Use .NET's built-in item-template engine rather than a custom console generator. The template does not auto-register endpoints and does not promise final REST route wording; generated endpoint routes are compile-ready starting points that must be reviewed during implementation.

**Open questions raised:** None new.

**Not done:** No NuGet template package, no automatic `Program.cs` edits, no route naming/casing transform, no generated EF/domain/migration files, and no changes to existing setup slices.

**Next:** A39 ADR-0003 scope-broadening docs are now top of the agent-executable queue.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - R01 generic respondent capture MVP

**Goal:** Add a narrow setup-serving respondent capture slice so the tenant setup workspace can prove the full front/back path from campaign draft to submitted generic answer, without adding platform-canonical OLBI content or broader launch machinery.

**Did:**

- Added `Platform.Domain.Responses` entities for `ResponseSession`, `Answer`, and JSON value validation.
- Added EF `response_session` and `answer` mappings plus `AddResponses` migration with tenant RLS, tenant guard triggers, and Docker-backed cross-tenant/read/write coverage.
- Added `Platform.Application.Features.Responses` contracts, handlers, endpoint mapping, API result helpers, and `ResponseCaptureStore`.
- Registered the response capture store and mapped respondent-capture endpoints from `Program.cs`.
- Added a setup-only Response Lab in `apps/web` that loads a campaign, creates a lab assignment/session, saves a simple answer, and submits it through the real API client.
- Updated `db-schema.md`, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, and `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md` so R01 is recorded accurately and the deferred pieces are explicit.

**Decisions:** R01 is a setup lab path, not a public launch/respondent product slice. Endpoints still require tenant/member context and tenant header. `anonymous_longitudinal` returns an explicit not-implemented validation result because participant-code hashing is not in scope. `answer` is currently a normal table with `(session_id, question_id)` uniqueness, not a partitioned parent; answer partitioning/retention automation remains future hardening.

**Verification:**

- `dotnet build Platform.slnx`
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj` - 66 passed
- `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'; dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj -p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false --verbosity minimal` - 93 passed
- `dotnet ef migrations has-pending-model-changes --project src/Platform.Infrastructure/Platform.Infrastructure.csproj --startup-project src/Platform.Infrastructure/Platform.Infrastructure.csproj --no-build` - no pending model changes
- `npm run lint --prefix apps/web`
- `npm run check --prefix apps/web`
- `npm run test --prefix apps/web -- --run` - 2 unit files / 5 tests and 9 Playwright tests passed
- `npm run build --prefix apps/web`
- `node --test tests/license-gate/check-licenses.test.mjs` - 8 passed
- `node tools/check-licenses.mjs` - checked 340 dependencies and wrote attribution artifacts

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-047 still blocks platform-canonical OLBI. Q-049/Q-050/Q-051/Q-053 remain open. Future slices still own public token landing, launch snapshots, consent records, participant-code hashing, scoring execution, reports/exports, offline/PWA sync, and production auth UX.

**Not done:** No public invitation-token resolver, no production respondent route, no launch snapshot, no consent enforcement, no Argon2id participant-code table/use, no anonymous-longitudinal capture, no scoring, no reports, no exports, no offline sync, no service worker, no SurveyJS runtime, no platform-canonical OLBI seeding, and no commit.

**Next:** Let owner review the R01 diff. If accepted, commit R01. After that there is still no unblocked agent-executable queue item except A49, which remains blocked by Q-047, unless the owner chooses/splits the next setup/product slice.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - S01 generic scoring execution MVP

**Goal:** Add the next narrow setup-serving proof slice after R01: compute and persist a simple score from a submitted setup-lab response session.

**Did:**

- Added a simple scoring evaluator for the setup scoring-rule document shape: `operations` with `mean` and `sum` over numeric question-code answers.
- Added `score_run` and `score` domain entities, EF mappings, migration, tenant RLS policies, tenant guard triggers, and Docker-backed RLS coverage.
- Added score computation contracts, MediatR command/handler, endpoint mapping, API result mapping, `IScoreComputationStore`, and real `ScoreComputationStore`.
- Registered the score store and mapped `POST /respondent/sessions/{sessionId}/scores`.
- Extended the setup workspace API client and Response Lab with a "Compute score" button and compact score output display.
- Updated `db-schema.md`, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, and the S01 plan docs.

**Decisions:** S01 is synchronous setup-lab scoring only. It chooses the latest published scoring rule for the campaign template, falling back to latest draft because GF05 readiness accepts draft rules. It persists a new score run on each compute. The evaluator supports only simple numeric JSON answers and `mean`/`sum`; this intentionally avoids the full ADR-0008 graph/registry/snapshot engine.

**Verification:**

- `dotnet build Platform.slnx` - build succeeded, 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj` - 74 passed
- `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'; dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj -p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false --verbosity minimal` - 99 passed
- `dotnet ef migrations has-pending-model-changes --project src/Platform.Infrastructure/Platform.Infrastructure.csproj --startup-project src/Platform.Infrastructure/Platform.Infrastructure.csproj --no-build` - no pending model changes
- `npm run lint --prefix apps/web`
- `npm run check --prefix apps/web`
- `npm run test --prefix apps/web -- --run` - 2 unit files / 5 tests and 9 Playwright tests passed
- `npm run build --prefix apps/web`
- `node --test tests/license-gate/check-licenses.test.mjs` - 8 passed
- `node tools/check-licenses.mjs` - checked 340 dependencies and wrote attribution artifacts

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-047 still blocks platform-canonical OLBI. Q-049/Q-050/Q-051/Q-053 remain open.

**Not done:** No full ADR-0008 operation graph, operation registry, rule validator, launch scoring snapshot, outbox/worker score execution, score replay/determinism suite, norm overlay, thresholds, dashboard/report/export projection, longitudinal delta, public respondent token flow, consent, participant-code hashing, offline sync, or platform-canonical OLBI fixtures.

**Next:** Merge S01 back to `main` if owner accepts. After that the generic proof loop is instrument -> template/questions -> campaign -> response -> score. The next useful owner-selected product slice is likely a minimal score/result view or public respondent token landing, depending on whether the owner wants more visible demo value or more production-like respondent flow.

**Not done:** No backend/API change, no validated result interpretation, no report/PDF/export, no thresholds, no norm overlay, no longitudinal delta, no public token flow, no consent, no participant-code hashing, no offline/PWA sync, and no platform-canonical OLBI content.

**Next:** After merge, the setup proof loop is visually readable through score result display. The next useful owner-selected slice is likely either public respondent token landing, launch snapshot/consent path, fuller ADR-0008 scoring validation, or report/export path depending on whether we prioritize production-like launch flow or demo/report value.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - L01 launch-lite campaign freeze

**Goal:** Add the smallest durable launch/freeze boundary for the setup proof path before public respondent URLs, invitations, consent, or production reports exist.

**Did:**

- Added L01 design and implementation plan docs under `docs/plans/`.
- Added `Campaign.Launch(...)` and `CampaignLaunchSnapshot` domain behavior.
- Added `campaign_launch_snapshot` EF mapping, migration, tenant RLS, tenant guard trigger, immutable update/delete trigger, and snapshot-aware DB guard updates for answers and score runs.
- Added `POST /campaigns/{id}/launch` setup command/API/store behavior.
- Launch now reruns readiness, freezes template/scoring/identity/default locale/readiness metadata, writes one snapshot per campaign, and moves the campaign to `live`.
- Updated response capture and setup-lab score computation to prefer the launch snapshot when one exists.
- Added setup workspace API/client and UI support for "Launch campaign" after readiness succeeds.
- Updated `db-schema.md`, `campaign-lifecycle.md`, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, and `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`.

**Decisions:** L01 is launch-lite only. It freezes the minimum fields needed by the current setup proof loop and deliberately keeps launch separate from invitation delivery. V1 seeding mixed launch state and notification sending; L01 keeps the freeze durable and leaves sending/public entry to later slices.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - P01 public open-link respondent entry

**Goal:** Add the smallest public respondent path after launch-lite: a tenant can create an anonymous open link for a launched campaign, and a respondent can open `/r/[token]`, answer, and submit without setup auth headers.

**Did:**

- Added `OpenLinkTokens` with `opn_{tenant_hint}_{random}` issuing, tenant-hint parsing, and SHA-256 token hashing.
- Added `POST /campaigns/{id}/open-link` for launched anonymous campaigns. The endpoint stores only the token hash and returns the raw token/`/r/{token}` path to the tenant.
- Added public token-scoped endpoints under `/respondent/open-links/{token}` for entry, session creation, answer save, and submit.
- Updated response capture to parse the tenant hint, set tenant RLS before lookup, verify the full token hash/channel, load the launch snapshot, and reject invalid/unknown links with neutral unavailable errors.
- Changed `response_session.assignment_id` from unique to non-unique so one reusable open-link assignment can collect multiple sessions; setup-lab session creation remains idempotent in application code.
- Added setup workspace UI to create an open link after launch and show the respondent path.
- Added a minimal `/r/[token]` respondent page that bypasses the setup/admin shell and uses a public API client with no dev tenant/user headers.
- Updated respondent/campaign/schema/handoff docs to record P01 and keep non-goals explicit.

**Decisions:** P01 supports launched `anonymous` campaigns only. Identified entry, anonymous-longitudinal participant-code entry, consent/disclosure enforcement, invitation batches/email sending, resume secrets, rate limiting/CAPTCHA, SurveyJS runtime, public score feedback, reports, exports, offline sync, and platform-canonical OLBI content remain out of scope.

**Verification:**

- `dotnet build Platform.slnx` - build succeeded, 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj --no-build` - 82 passed
- `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'; dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj -p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false --verbosity minimal` - 116 passed

- `npm run check --prefix apps/web` - 0 errors, 0 warnings
- `npm run test --prefix apps/web -- --run` - 2 unit files / 5 tests and 10 Playwright tests passed

## 2026-05-07 - codex - A49a generic scoring fixture replay harness

**Goal:** Add a CI-friendly scoring fixture replay path that is fully non-canonical and synthetic, so the current S01 engine can be checked for drift without touching OLBI rights, canonical item text, or platform-shipped instrument fixtures.

**Did:**

- Added three JSON fixtures under `fixtures/instruments/dummy-burnout/noncanonical/`: happy path, multi-output, and expected missing-answer failure.
- Added `ScoringFixtureReplayTests` to discover non-canonical fixture JSON files, validate fixture invariants, replay them through `SimpleScoringEngine.Evaluate`, and compare outputs with a numeric tolerance.
- Updated scoring spec and handoff docs so A49a is done and A49b remains the OLBI/canonical fixture path blocked by Q-047.

**Decisions:** A49a deliberately uses synthetic JSON fixtures because the current S01 scoring engine consumes simple JSON `mean`/`sum` operations. Canonical platform fixtures remain YAML per the spec, require source evidence, and stay under A49b/Q-047.

**Verification:**

## 2026-05-07 - codex - C01 consent/disclosure MVP

**Goal:** Add the minimal anonymous open-link consent/disclosure path after P01 without pulling in the full ethics, retention, withdrawal, or legal-template system.

**Did:**

- Added `ConsentDocument` and `ConsentRecord` domain entities, EF mappings, migration, tenant RLS policies, tenant guards, and launch snapshot `consent_document_id`.
- Setup campaign-series creation now creates a default published consent document; launch readiness blocks when a campaign series has no usable consent document for the campaign locale.
- Launch snapshots freeze the consent document id.
- Public open-link entry now returns the frozen consent document.
- Public open-link session creation requires the accepted consent document id and all required grants, creates a `consent_record`, and links `response_session.consent_record_id`.
- Public open-link submit rejects sessions missing a consent record.
- `/r/[token]` now renders the disclosure first, hides questions until required grants are accepted, and sends accepted grants when creating the session.
- Updated `consent-and-ethics.md`, `respondent-ui.md`, `campaign-lifecycle.md`, and handoff docs to record C01 and its non-goals.

**Decisions:** C01 is anonymous open-link only. It deliberately does not implement legal template libraries, tenant legal review, retention/disclosure policy records, withdrawal, re-consent, consent PDFs, identified public entry, anonymous-longitudinal participant-code consent, invitation delivery, or platform-canonical OLBI content.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - C02 retention/disclosure policy foundation

**Goal:** Add the missing policy provenance foundation after C01 so invitations, reports/exports, and fuller scoring are no longer built on an undefined retention/disclosure boundary.

**Did:**

- Added `RetentionPolicy` and `DisclosurePolicy` domain entities with validation for allowed retention anchors/actions, disclosure strategies, JSON shapes, retirement windows, and usability checks.
- Added EF mappings, migration, RLS policies, tenant guard triggers, and `campaign_launch_snapshot.retention_policy_id` / `campaign_launch_snapshot.disclosure_policy_id`.
- Setup campaign-series creation now creates proof default retention/disclosure policies.
- Launch readiness blocks when a campaign has no usable retention or disclosure policy.
- Launch snapshots and launch responses freeze policy ids; the setup UI shows the frozen policy provenance.
- Updated consent/ethics, campaign lifecycle, DB schema, and handoff docs.

**Decisions:** Defaults are proof defaults only: `retain_for_years = 1`, `response_submitted_at`, `anonymize`, `k_min = 5`, and `hide_cell`. They are not legal advice, not final tenant-facing commitments, and not a production retention schedule. Q-053 still controls final GDPR/legal package, final retention numbers, external wording, and legal templates.

**Verification:**

## 2026-05-07 - codex - S03 scoring execution subset

**Goal:** Start replacing the S01 `mean`/`sum` shortcut with a narrow ADR-0008-style graph evaluator before report/export work depends on scoring semantics.

**Did:**

- Added S03 design and implementation plans under `docs/plans/`.
- Extended `SimpleScoringEngine.Evaluate(...)` to execute graph documents with `inputs`, `nodes`, and `outputs` while preserving legacy `operations` documents.
- Added graph operation support for `select_answers`, `reverse_code`, `mean`, `sum`, `count_valid`, and `subscale_aggregate` with `mean`/`sum`.
- Added `require_all` and `min_valid_count` missing-data policies.
- Added unit coverage for graph mean, reverse coding, count-valid, partial missing-data scoring, unknown node errors, and legacy compatibility.
- Added a non-canonical synthetic graph fixture under `fixtures/instruments/dummy-burnout/noncanonical/`.
- Added Docker-backed `ScoreComputationStore` coverage proving reverse-coded graph scoring persists expected `score` rows.
- Updated the setup workspace default scoring document and Playwright coverage so the setup proof posts the graph shape.
- Updated scoring spec and handoff docs.

**Decisions:** S03 is the first execution subset, not the full ADR-0008 engine. It keeps the public evaluator API stable and intentionally leaves richer validation, editor UX, reporting, and canonical fixture sourcing for later slices.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - E01 provider-safe email delivery proof

**Goal:** Replace the C03 queued-only email invitation proof with provider-safe local/dev delivery processing while keeping raw invite paths out of durable storage.

**Did:**

- Added `Notification.MarkSent(...)`, `Notification.MarkFailed(...)`, and `InvitationToken.ReissueHash(...)`.
- Added notification delivery application contracts, command, validator, endpoint, and `INotificationDeliveryStore`.
- Added `IEmailDeliveryProvider`, local/dev delivery sink, and config-gated SMTP provider boundary.
- Added `notification_delivery_attempt` domain/EF/migration/RLS/tenant guard support.
- Added `NotificationDeliveryStore` processing for queued email invitation notifications, with invitation-token hash reissue at send time and sent/failed status transitions.
- Added tenant-member `POST /campaigns/{id}/notification-deliveries/process`.
- Added setup-workspace "Process local delivery" proof and frontend API client coverage.
- Updated roadmap/handoff docs so P02 is the next active proof-spine slice.

**Decisions:** E01 does not store raw invite tokens or respondent paths in notification, outbox, or delivery-attempt rows. The raw `/r/inv_...` path exists only in the provider message and setup proof response. The default provider is local/dev; SMTP is config-gated through environment/secrets and is not a production deliverability commitment.

**Verification:** Ran filtered domain, application, provider, model/RLS, API, frontend unit, and Playwright tests. The Docker processor test passed with `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'`.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-047 still blocks platform-canonical OLBI. Q-049/Q-050/Q-051/Q-053 remain open.

**Not done:** No production deliverability operations, SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup, reminders, bounce/complaint/unsubscribe/suppression handling, tenant email template editor, identified invitations, anonymous-longitudinal invitations, marketing email, production legal copy, P02 participant-code implementation, or P03 public anonymous-longitudinal entry was added.

**Next:** Start P02 participant-code foundation with Argon2id per campaign series unless the owner changes priority.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - P02 participant-code foundation

**Goal:** Add the anonymous-longitudinal participant-code persistence and Argon2id hashing foundation without building public anonymous-longitudinal entry, wave UI, trajectory reports, exports, withdrawal, or recovery.

**Did:**

- Added `ParticipantCode` domain model and EF mapping for `participant_code`.
- Added Argon2id hashing contracts/implementation using `campaign_series.code_salt`, ADR-0005 default parameters, and stored parameter provenance.
- Added `participant_code` migration SQL with tenant RLS, same-tenant campaign-series guard, unique `(campaign_series_id, hash)`, and response-session participant-code guard coverage.
- Added neutral lookup-or-create application/store boundary that returns participant-code metadata only and stores no raw or normalized code.
- Removed the obsolete launch-readiness warning that participant-code persistence is missing.
- Updated roadmap, schema, feature, and handoff docs so P03 is the active next slice.

**Decisions:** P02 is foundation only. Public anonymous-longitudinal entry remains P03. The platform now has the durable participant-code table/hash/store boundary, but no `/r/[token]` participant-code step, no public consent/session wiring for anonymous-longitudinal campaigns, no duplicate-submit policy enforcement, no trajectory report/export, and no withdrawal/recovery flow.

## 2026-05-07 - codex - P03 anonymous-longitudinal public entry

**Goal:** Wire participant-code resolution into the public respondent path so launched `anonymous_longitudinal` campaigns can collect responses without identity or raw-code storage.

**Did:**

- Allowed launched `anonymous_longitudinal` campaigns to create public open-link tokens.
- Added `requiresParticipantCode` to public entry and `participantCode` to public session creation.
- Added a participant-code step to `/r/{token}` only when the entry requires it.
- Resolved participant codes through the P02 store and stored only `response_session.participant_code_id`.
- Rejected participant-code payloads for plain anonymous campaigns.
- Enforced one submitted response per campaign/code at public session creation and again before submit.
- Added a P03 design and implementation plan under `docs/plans/`.
- Updated rights-boundary docs and ignored local `.dotnet/`/`*.binlog` artifacts in a follow-up housekeeping commit.

**Decisions:** P03 is public entry/session integration only. It does not implement two-wave proof UI, wave-comparison reporting, trajectory export, withdrawal/recovery, production respondent runtime hardening, or platform-canonical OLBI content. Email invitation batches remain anonymous-only.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-047 still blocks platform-canonical OLBI/A49b. Q-049/Q-050/Q-051/Q-053 remain open.

**Not done:** No two-wave campaign-series proof path, no wave-comparison report, no anonymous-longitudinal trajectory export, no withdrawal/recovery, no production respondent runtime hardening, and no platform-canonical OLBI content.

## 2026-05-08 - codex - R03 wave-comparison aggregate report proof

**Goal:** Turn W01's two-wave anonymous-longitudinal proof into a buyer-visible aggregate wave-comparison report proof without exposing individual trajectories or raw answers.

**Did:**

- Added tenant-member `GET /campaign-series/{id}/wave-comparison-proof`.
- Added a read-only wave-comparison proof store over the first two launched `anonymous_longitudinal` waves in a campaign series.
- Returns proof status, not-validated interpretation status, baseline/comparison wave provenance, selected disclosure policy, per-dimension side-by-side means, linked-pair counts, aggregate deltas, and paired delta means.
- Gates all values and deltas behind the stricter frozen disclosure `k_min` across both wave submitted counts and complete linked-pair counts.
- Added `ScoringRuleCompatibilityResolver` for same-rule detection and minimal semver-range metadata evaluation across `output_equivalent_with`, `descriptive_only_with`, and `incompatible_with`.
- Blocks deltas for missing/incompatible mixed-version metadata while keeping side-by-side means visible when disclosure passes.
- Added setup-workspace API client types, "View wave comparison proof" action, compact proof panel, and e2e coverage.
- Updated roadmap/handoff/domain docs so R03 is done and X02 is next.

**Decisions:** R03 is aggregate proof only. It compares the first two launched anonymous-longitudinal waves and uses materialized score rows. It does not create raw exports, individual trajectory reports, object storage/downloads, dashboards/PDFs, validated interpretation, norms, thresholds, respondent runtime hardening, or platform-canonical OLBI fixtures.

## 2026-05-08 - codex - U01 respondent runtime hardening

**Goal:** Harden the existing public `/r/{token}` proof route for mobile/error/retry behavior without widening into SurveyJS runtime, offline sync, production auth, dashboards, exports, or platform-canonical OLBI work.

**Did:**

- Added design/plan docs: `../../plans/2026-05-08-u01-respondent-runtime-hardening-design.md` and `../../plans/2026-05-08-u01-respondent-runtime-hardening.md`.
- Split fatal entry-load errors from consent/session/submit action errors.
- Added entry-load retry with a `Try again` control.
- Kept consent/session failures inside the consent step with accessible inline alerts.
- Added required-answer and numeric scale validation before public save/submit calls.
- Covered submit retry after failed save while preserving answer values.
- Added mobile viewport coverage for anonymous-longitudinal participant-code entry and public submit.
- Updated respondent UI, backlog, current roadmap, NEXT-ACTIONS, and NEXT-SESSION-PLAN so U01 is done and AU01 is next.

**Decisions:** U01 is frontend runtime hardening over existing public respondent APIs. It does not change backend identity-mode semantics, token storage, consent storage, participant-code hashing, scoring, reports, exports, or launch behavior. AU01 is next, but it still intersects Q-020, so the production auth provider decision must not be assumed closed.

## 2026-05-08 - codex - AU01 provider-neutral auth/session UX boundary

**Goal:** Replace the setup workspace's implicit development-auth assumption with an explicit provider-neutral session boundary, without choosing Auth0 vs Keycloak or implementing provider login/callback/session storage.

**Did:**

- Added design/plan docs: `../../plans/2026-05-08-au01-auth-session-ux-boundary-design.md` and `../../plans/2026-05-08-au01-auth-session-ux-boundary.md`.
- Added typed setup API client support for `GET /auth/session`.
- Gated the tenant setup workspace behind `/auth/session` before protected setup controls render.
- Added checking, sign-in-required, tenant-access-unavailable, and session-check-failed states.
- Kept development auth headers behind `PUBLIC_DEV_AUTH_ENABLED=true`.
- Added provider-neutral `PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGIN_URL` and `PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGOUT_URL` placeholders.
- Updated setup/app-shell Playwright coverage, README frontend env docs, auth architecture notes, backlog, current roadmap, NEXT-ACTIONS, and NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.

**Decisions:** AU01 is the provider-neutral boundary only. Q-020 remains open for Auth0 vs Keycloak and any provider-specific login/callback/session-store work. DEP01 is next because the proof path still needs a local-to-staging deployment proof.

## 2026-05-08 - codex - DEP01-A local Docker deployment proof

**Goal:** Prove the current tenant-private proof spine can run as a local staging-shaped Docker stack without touching the owner's VPS or claiming production readiness.

**Did:**

- Added design/plan docs: `../../plans/2026-05-08-dep01a-local-docker-deployment-proof-design.md` and `../../plans/2026-05-08-dep01a-local-docker-deployment-proof.md`.

- Added `deploy/staging` API, web, and migrator Dockerfiles.

- Added safe local `env.example` defaults and start/smoke/stop PowerShell wrappers.
- Switched `apps/web` to `@sveltejs/adapter-node` for the Docker proof runtime.
- Fixed the stop script to recover Docker Desktop's default `resources\bin` path when the current shell does not have `docker` on `PATH`.
- Updated README, web README, deployment docs, roadmap, backlog, and handoff docs so DEP01-A is done and DEP01-B is next.

**Decisions:** DEP01-A is local Docker only. It intentionally uses development auth and local proof secrets. It is not VPS staging, production Docker hardening, worker deployment, registry, GitHub Actions deploy, TLS/reverse proxy, secret management, backup/restore, observability, or provider-specific production auth.

**Verification:**

- `npm --prefix apps/web run build` - passed with `@sveltejs/adapter-node`.

- `deploy/staging/smoke-local-staging.ps1` - `Local staging smoke passed.`
- `deploy/staging/stop-local-staging.ps1 -RemoveVolumes` - stopped containers and removed the proof volume.

**Not done:** No real VPS staging, production hardening, worker container, registry, CI deploy, TLS/reverse proxy, production secrets, backup/restore, observability dashboards, development-auth removal, provider-specific auth, or production smoke gate.

**Next:** DEP01-B VPS staging adaptation, with Q-020 and Q-021 still visible.

## 2026-05-08 - codex - DEP01-B portable VPS staging adaptation

**Goal:** Adapt the DEP01-A local Docker proof into a portable nginx/subdomain VPS staging pack without touching live VPS resources or continuing into production deployment hardening.

**Did:**

- Added `deploy/staging/vps.env.example` with provider-neutral staging variables and placeholder-only secrets.
- Added `deploy/staging/docker-compose.vps.yml` with loopback-only API/web host bindings and Postgres host port removal.
- Added `deploy/staging/nginx.example.conf` for `staging.example.com` and `staging-api.example.com` reverse-proxy routing.
- Added `docs/v2/40-ops/vps-staging-runbook.md` with manual DNS/nginx/compose/smoke/stop/rollback guidance.
- Added `tests/deployment-package/vps-staging-package.test.mjs` to lock the portable VPS package contract.
- Updated README, deployment docs, roadmap, backlog, NEXT-ACTIONS, and NEXT-SESSION-PLAN so DEP01-B is done and deployment work pauses by owner decision.

**Decisions:** Subdomain routing is the default. nginx remains the assumed public ingress because the owner's VPS already uses nginx. The VPS pack is provider-neutral and should not become Hostinger-specific. DEP01-C/GitHub Actions is deferred until the owner explicitly reopens deployment automation.

**Verification:**

- `node --test tests/deployment-package/vps-staging-package.test.mjs` - 5 passed.
- `docker compose --env-file deploy/staging/vps.env.example -f deploy/staging/docker-compose.yml -f deploy/staging/docker-compose.vps.yml config` - confirmed API/web publish only on `127.0.0.1` and no Postgres published host port.

**Not done:** No live VPS rollout, nginx site activation, DNS changes, TLS certificate issuance, production auth, production secrets manager, backup/restore drill, GitHub Actions deploy, registry/image promotion, observability wiring, worker deployment, or paid-tenant readiness.

**Next:** D23 post-DEP01-B product/runtime reassessment. Do not continue staging/deployment work unless the owner explicitly reopens it.

## 2026-05-09 - codex - UX11 selected-series operations workflow foundation

**Goal:** Make `/app/campaign-series/{id}/operations` use a primary
selected-series operations workflow for launch-readiness, launch, open-link,
invitation-batch, and local/dev delivery actions instead of relying on the
generic proof workbench.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-09-ux11-selected-series-operations-workflow.md`.
- Added `apps/web/src/lib/product/operations-workflow.ts` and focused unit
  coverage for operations action availability.
- Added `apps/web/src/lib/product/SelectedSeriesOperationsWorkflow.svelte`.
- Updated `SelectedSeriesSurface.svelte` so the operations route renders the
  operations workflow and no longer mounts `ProofWorkflowSurface`.
- The workflow targets `operationsWorkspace.selectedCampaign` and calls the
  existing setup API client methods for launch readiness, launch, open-link
  creation, invitation batch creation, and local/dev delivery processing.
- Successful actions refresh
  `GET /campaign-series/{id}/operations-workspace`; failures remain local and
  retryable.
- Transient open-link paths, invitation recipients, respondent paths, provider
  labels, and delivery proof outputs stay in local proof/local action result UI
  rather than durable product read models.
- Updated product-surface Playwright coverage for primary operations workflow
  rendering, selected-campaign endpoint targeting, workspace refreshes, and
  removal of the operations proof workbench.
- Updated roadmap/handoff docs so UX11 is recorded as done and `D35`
  post-UX11 product/runtime assessment is the next queue head.

**Decisions:** UX11 is a route workflow foundation, not production operations.
It makes launch/open-link/invitation/local-delivery actions product-native on
the operations route while keeping local/dev delivery and raw action outputs
clearly proof/local.

**Explicitly not done:** No production SMTP/provider deliverability hardening,
no reminders, no bounce/complaint/unsubscribe handling, no tenant email
templates, no full recipient management/imports, no durable raw respondent-path
or raw recipient redisplay, no final operations console, no report/wave
dashboard UX, no charts, no PDFs, no norms, no thresholds, no validated
interpretation, no raw exports, no trajectory exports, no production auth, no
deployment automation, and no platform-canonical OLBI fixture/content work.

respondent/admin shell isolation.

switching, public score feedback, withdrawal/re-consent, production

## 2026-05-09 - codex - U03 respondent runtime productization foundation

**Goal:** Make the public `/r/{token}` respondent route feel like a clearer
product runtime by separating answer save/review from final submit over the
existing public entry/session/save/submit APIs.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-09-u03-respondent-runtime-productization.md`.
- Updated `/r/[token]` so SurveyJS answer capture uses `Save and review`
  instead of directly submitting.
- Added a review state that shows saved answer count and session provenance.
- Added `Back to edit` so respondents can return to the SurveyJS step without
  losing answers.
- Added `Submit reviewed response` so final submit only happens from the
  reviewed/saved session.
- Kept save and submit failures local/retryable and kept answers preserved.
- Updated respondent route E2E coverage for save/review, validation, retry,
  mobile sizing, and setup/admin auth-header isolation.
- Updated `current-roadmap.md`, `frontend-product-surfaces.md`,
  `respondent-ui.md`, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, and `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md` so U03 is
  done and D43 is the next assessment.

**Decisions:** U03 remains frontend-only over the existing public contracts:
`GET /respondent/open-links/{token}`,
`POST /respondent/open-links/{token}/sessions`,
`PUT /respondent/open-links/{token}/sessions/{sessionId}/answers`, and
`POST /respondent/open-links/{token}/sessions/{sessionId}/submit`.

**Explicitly not done:** No full offline/PWA sync, autosave, resume secrets,
URL-token scrubbing, identified entry, full branching runtime, locale switching,
public score feedback, withdrawal/re-consent, production rate limiting/CAPTCHA,
production deliverability, reports/exports, production auth/deployment,
SurveyJS Creator/designer, or platform-canonical OLBI content.

src/lib/respondent/surveyjs-adapter.test.ts src/lib/api/setup.test.ts`

## 2026-05-09 - codex - X04 governed campaign-series response export proof

**Goal:** Add a proof-only governed campaign-series response CSV/codebook export
over submitted sessions, without turning the export path into production object
storage, signed URLs, async workers, PDFs, raw operational dumps, or identified
subject export.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-09-x04-governed-response-export.md`.
- Added `campaign_series_response_csv_codebook` as an accepted artifact type and
  added the EF migration `AddResponseExportArtifactType`.
- Added tenant-member `POST /campaign-series/{campaignSeriesId}/response-exports`.
- Implemented synchronous DB-backed response export generation in
  `ReportProofExportStore`.
- Exported submitted sessions only, with artifact-local `response_row_id`,
  artifact-local `trajectory_id` for anonymous-longitudinal rows when disclosure
  allows, campaign/wave/template/scoring provenance, consent/retention/
  disclosure provenance, locale/timing metadata, and item answer columns.
- Excluded operational identifiers from CSV output: assignment ids,
  response-session ids, token material, delivery recipients, participant-code
  ids/hashes/raw codes, IP hashes, user-agent hashes, and subject ids.
- Extended the reports workspace registry to include both aggregate report-proof
  and campaign-series response-export artifacts.
- Added a separate `Create response export` action to the selected-series
  Reports route workflow and made fetch/download use the selected/new artifact.
- Updated research export, frontend product surface, roadmap, and handoff docs
  so X04 is done and D44 is next.

**Known narrowness:** `export_artifact.campaign_id` is still required, so X04
anchors the response export to the latest submitted campaign while
`campaign_series_id` remains the semantic target. A later lifecycle/schema slice
should decide whether series-only artifacts need a nullable-campaign shape.

**Verification:**

- RED domain test failed before adding the artifact type:
  `ExportArtifactTypes.CampaignSeriesResponseCsvCodebook` was missing.
- RED API test failed with 404 before adding the response export endpoint.
- RED Docker projection test failed with `response_export.not_implemented`, then
  with `ck_export_artifact_type` before the migration.

"FullyQualifiedName~ExportArtifactTests" --verbosity minimal` passed with 5
  tests.
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj
--filter
"FullyQualifiedName~Campaign_series_response_export_endpoint_returns_artifact|FullyQualifiedName~Export_artifact_model_maps"
--verbosity minimal` passed with 2 tests.

tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj
-p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false --filter

- `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'; dotnet test

-p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false --filter

## 2026-05-09 - codex - X06 export artifact lifecycle foundation

**Goal:** Add narrow lifecycle semantics to `export_artifact` before object
storage, signed URLs, async workers, PDFs, charting, or production export
workflow automation.

**Did:**

- Extended `ExportArtifact` with `queued`, `rendering`, `succeeded`, `failed`,
  `expired`, and `deleted` states; lifecycle timestamps; privacy-safe failure
  reason codes; nullable checksum/content for non-succeeded rows; and
  `CanDownload`.
- Updated EF mapping, constraints, migration, model snapshot, and migration
  script assertions. Existing artifacts are backfilled to `succeeded`.
- Updated report export retrieval/download behavior so non-downloadable
  artifacts return `export_artifact.not_downloadable` instead of content.
- Exposed lifecycle fields and `canDownload` through report export DTOs,
  product read models, and selected-series Reports registry rows.
- Updated the frontend Reports dashboard and workflow to render lifecycle rows,
  nullable checksums, downloadable state, and CSV download gating while keeping
  metadata fetch available.
- Updated roadmap/handoff/research-export/frontend docs so X06 is complete and
  D46 is the next queue head.

**Decision:** X06 is lifecycle foundation only. Existing aggregate proof and
campaign-series response proof exporters still write immediate `succeeded`
artifacts synchronously. Non-succeeded artifacts can now be represented and
inspected as metadata, but they are not downloadable.

## 2026-05-09 - codex - BUILD-001 local route-owned frontend bundle gate

**Goal:** Turn the A42 bundle-budget contract into a local checker after UX19
introduced real ECharts/dashboard weight.

**Did:**

- Added `apps/web/scripts/check-bundle-budgets.mjs`.
- Added `npm run check:bundles` to `apps/web/package.json`.
- Added focused unit coverage in
  `apps/web/src/lib/build-gates/bundle-budget-checker.test.js`.
- The checker reads SvelteKit/Vite production build output, maps server routes
  to client chunks, measures gzip JS/CSS, hard-fails forbidden first-load
  dependency leaks, checks configured hard lazy dependency leaks, prints a route
  budget table, and writes ignored `artifacts/bundle-budgets/report.json`.
- Bootstrap dynamic imports from SvelteKit's entry app are ignored for route
  ownership so unrelated route chunks do not make every route look like it owns
  every lazy branch.
- Updated build-gate, roadmap, frontend-surface, dashboard/report,
  research-export, README, backlog, NEXT-ACTIONS, and NEXT-SESSION-PLAN docs so
  BUILD-001 is done and D48 is the active queue head.

**Decision:** BUILD-001 is local first-load route enforcement only. The current
hard gates pass after a production build. Setup and operations still warn on
conditional lazy ECharts references advertised from shared selected-series
chunks; those warnings need Playwright route-smoke request proof or a component
split before becoming hard failures.

**Explicitly not selected:** No GitHub Actions/DEP01-C, CI artifact upload,
live VPS rollout, production auth/deployment, PDFs, object storage, signed
URLs, async workers, full production export lifecycle, validated
interpretation, norms, thresholds, deeper respondent runtime, production
deliverability, or platform-canonical OLBI content.

**Verification:**

  initially failed because the checker script did not exist.
- RED regression: a fake SvelteKit entry-app dynamic import caused unrelated
  ECharts chunks to be counted against the respondent route.
- GREEN: `npm run test:unit -- --run src/lib/build-gates/bundle-budget-checker.test.js`
  passed with 1 file / 4 tests.
- `npm run check` initially failed because the new `.mjs` script and JS test
  were inside the checked project and needed JSDoc types; after typing the
  checker, `npm run check` passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings.
- `npm run test:unit -- --run` passed with 16 files / 93 tests.
- `npm run build` passed; Vite still emitted the existing non-failing
  large-chunk warning.
- `npm run check:bundles` passed. Current report: respondent entry 46.4 KB
  gzip JS, selected reports/waves 87.2 KB gzip JS, all configured hard gates
  pass; two conditional lazy ECharts warnings remain for setup/operations.
- Targeted Prettier check for the touched web files passed.
- `git diff --check` passed with Git LF/CRLF warnings only.

## 2026-05-10 - codex - U04 respondent autosave and dirty-state hardening

**Goal:** Harden the current public `/r/[token]` respondent route against answer
loss and stale review state without widening into offline sync, resume handles,
URL-token scrubbing, backend/API changes, or production respondent runtime.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-10-u04-respondent-autosave-dirty-state.md`.
- Added route-local save status to `/r/[token]`: `idle`, `dirty`, `saving`,
  `saved`, and `failed`, with visible respondent copy.
- Added autosave after answer changes once a public response session exists,
  using the existing open-link answer save endpoint.
- Reused the same save helper for autosave and manual `Save and review`, so
  failed autosaves preserve answers and can be retried manually.
- Cleared stale review state when edited answers differ from the reviewed saved
  answer set.
- Added a `beforeunload` guard only while the session has dirty/saving/failed
  unsaved answers.
- Updated respondent, frontend-surface, roadmap, backlog, and handoff docs so
  U04 is done and D52 is the next assessment gate.

**Explicitly not done:** No offline IndexedDB sync, resume secrets, tokenless
public session handles, URL-token scrubbing, identified entry, full branching
runtime, locale switching, public score feedback, withdrawal/re-consent, rate
limiting/CAPTCHA, production deliverability, production auth/deployment, PDFs,
object storage, signed URLs, async workers, validated interpretation, norms,

closure.
