## 2026-05-10 - codex - U05 respondent same-browser server-draft resume foundation

**Goal:** Make U04 autosave useful across reload/revisit for the same browser
without widening into offline sync, tokenless public session handles, URL-token
scrubbing, or cross-device resume.

**Did:**

- Added public draft-read API support:
  `GET /respondent/open-links/{token}/sessions/{sessionId}/draft`.
- Added `OpenLinkSessionDraftResponse` and `SavedAnswerResponse` contracts,
  MediatR query/validator/handler, endpoint mapping, and fake-store API
  coverage proving no setup/admin auth headers are required.
- Implemented token/session-bound Postgres draft reads in `ResponseCaptureStore`
  by reusing the existing open-link token/session assignment guard before
  reading saved answers.
- Added Docker-backed store coverage for restoring saved answer values and for
  rejecting a session pointer from another open-link token with neutral
  `response_session.not_found`.
- Added frontend API client types/method and `/r/[token]` restore behavior:
  after session creation the route stores only
  `respondent-session:{assignmentId} = response_session_id` in
  `sessionStorage`; after entry load it fetches the draft, restores answers,
  restores submitted receipt state, and clears stale pointers silently.
- Added Playwright coverage for reload restore, stale-pointer fallback, and
  submitted-draft receipt restore.
- Updated respondent, frontend-surface, dashboard/report, research-export,
  roadmap, backlog, and handoff docs so U05 is done and D53 is the next
  assessment gate.

**Explicitly not done:** No offline IndexedDB sync, service-worker draft
queues, cross-device resume, tokenless public session handles, URL-token
scrubbing, identified entry, full branching runtime, locale switching, public
score feedback, withdrawal/re-consent, rate limiting/CAPTCHA, production
deliverability, production auth/deployment, PDFs, object storage, signed URLs,
async workers, full production export lifecycle, validated interpretation,
norms, thresholds, platform-canonical OLBI, official OLBI score labels, or
Q-047 closure.

scrub the raw token from browser history, use the handle for save/draft/submit
where possible, keep stale/invalid handle failures neutral, and preserve
public-call auth isolation.

## 2026-05-10 - codex - U06 respondent tokenless session handle and URL-scrubbing foundation

**Goal:** Remove raw open-link/invitation token exposure from the steady-state
public respondent URL/API flow after a response session exists, without adding
offline sync, cross-device resume, account login, identified entry, production
auth/deployment, PDFs, or platform-canonical OLBI work.

**Did:**

- Added U06 design and implementation plans:
  `docs/plans/2026-05-10-u06-respondent-tokenless-session-handle-design.md`
  and `docs/plans/2026-05-10-u06-respondent-tokenless-session-handle.md`.
- Added `OpenLinkSessionHandles` for `rsh_...` public response-session handles:
  tenant routing hint, SHA-256 hash, and neutral malformed-handle errors.
- Extended `response_session` with nullable `public_handle_hash` and
  `public_handle_issued_at`, plus filtered unique index and hash-shape check
  constraint.
- Updated open-link/invitation session creation to issue a public handle and
  store only the handle hash.
- Added public-handle endpoints:
  `GET /respondent/public-sessions/{handle}/draft`,
  `PUT /respondent/public-sessions/{handle}/answers`, and
  `POST /respondent/public-sessions/{handle}/submit`.
- Added handle resolution under tenant RLS with anonymous assignment, live
  campaign, launch snapshot, and invitation-token channel/expiry guards.
- Updated `/r/[token]` so raw `opn_...`/`inv_...` tokens are initial-entry
  credentials only. After session creation or handle restore, the route stores
  a same-browser `{ sessionId, publicHandle }` pointer, replaces the active URL
  with `/r/{publicHandle}`, and uses public-handle endpoints for draft/save/
  submit where possible.
- Preserved U05 compatibility: old bare `response_session_id` pointers still
  fall back to the token-bound draft endpoint, and API responses without

## 2026-05-13 - codex - S06 submitted-response score materialization foundation

**Goal:** Materialize score rows automatically after successful setup-lab,
open-link, and public-handle submit so reports, exports, and waves no longer
depend on a per-session call to the explicit score endpoint.

**Did:**

- Added S06 design and implementation plans under `docs/plans/`.
- Added `SubmittedResponseScoreMaterializer` and routed both
  `ResponseCaptureStore.SubmitSessionAsync` and
  `ScoreComputationStore.ComputeResponseScoresAsync` through it.
- Submit-time scoring now runs inside the existing tenant transaction after
  `session.Submit(...)` and before `SaveChangesAsync(...)`.
- Unscored instruments still submit without score rows. Invalid configured
  scoring rules return explicit scoring errors and do not commit the submit.
- The manual score endpoint now returns an existing successful materialization
  instead of inserting duplicate score runs or rows.
- Updated product-spine smoke so `Submit-LinkedResponse` no longer calls
  `/respondent/sessions/{id}/scores`; report, export, and wave assertions run
  before a single manual score endpoint compatibility proof.
- Updated handoff/source docs and promoted `D70` as the next queue item.

**V1 reference review:** Checked read-only v1 `../AnketasProject`
`ResponseService`, `ResponseRepository`, `EvaluationController`, and statistics
repository/procedure references. V1 stores answers directly on response rows and
statistics read/procedure paths calculate from those submitted answers rather
than requiring a separate per-response score endpoint. S06 ports the product
lesson, not the v1 implementation: v2 still keeps durable score rows for
governed reports/exports, but submit now makes those rows product-natural.

**Decisions:** Keep S06 synchronous and transaction-scoped. Do not add an async
score worker or outbox scoring flow until that is selected separately.

**Open questions raised:** None.

**Explicitly not done:** No platform-canonical OLBI fixtures, public item text,
official labels, Q-047 closure, norms, thresholds, validated interpretation,
new scoring operations, dashboard redesign, PDFs, object storage, signed URLs,
async score workers, async export workers, production auth/deployment,
retention/withdrawal policy, or full offline/PWA runtime.

## 2026-05-13 - codex - S07 scoring-rule launch safety preview foundation

**Goal:** Harden launch readiness so submit-time scoring cannot be enabled for a
campaign whose selected scoring rule references missing selected-template item
codes or fails deterministic execution through the current scoring engine.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-13-s07-scoring-rule-launch-safety-preview.md`.
- Added `ScoringRuleLaunchPreview` to extract legacy `operations[*].items` and
  graph answer-input item codes, compare them with selected template question
  codes, and run deterministic preview through `SimpleScoringEngine`.
- Wired launch readiness in `SetupWorkflowStore` to return privacy-safe
  blockers `scoring_rule.item_code_missing` and
  `scoring_rule.preview_failed`.
- Added unit tests for missing graph/legacy item codes, valid preview
  execution, and engine-failure wrapping.
- Added Docker-backed integration coverage for launch readiness blocking a
  missing item code and accepting a valid preview.
- Marked `S07` done and promoted `D71` as the next queue item in
  `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, `backlog.md`, and
  `current-roadmap.md`.
- Updated scoring, lifecycle, dashboard/report, export, and respondent-runtime
  docs to record the S07 launch-safety boundary.

**V1 reference review:** Checked read-only v1 `../AnketasProject`
`SurveyStructureController`, `StatisticsRepository`,
`EvaluationLifecycleController`, and `StatisticsScoringContractTests`. No v1
launch-time scoring preview equivalent was found. Useful lesson: v1 statistics
depend on hardwired question ids/procedures and guards against deleting
question structure after responses exist, so v2 needs explicit pre-launch
rule/template consistency instead of discovering score breakage in downstream
statistics or respondent submit.

**Decisions:** Keep S07 narrow. Launch readiness performs selected-rule item
code resolution and current-engine deterministic preview only. It does not add

## 2026-05-13 - codex - OPS02 selected-series score coverage monitor foundation

**Goal:** Add tenant-scoped, aggregate-only score coverage visibility for
selected campaign series after submit-time scoring and launch safety.

**Did:**

- Added
  `docs/plans/2026-05-13-ops02-score-coverage-monitor.md` with acceptance
  criteria, v1 reference review, TDD plan, and verification plan.
- Added `ScoreCoverageSummary` and unit coverage for complete, partial,
  not-configured, no-submissions, latest-activity, and mixed-not-configured
  states.
- Extended Operations and Reports selected-series contracts with
  `CampaignSeriesScoreCoverageResponse`.
- Extended Operations campaign rows with scoring rule id, scored submitted
  count, unscored submitted count, not-configured submitted count, latest
  scoring activity, and coverage status.
- Extended `ProductSurfaceReadStore` to aggregate submitted response-session
  score coverage from successful `ScoreRun` rows joined through submitted
  response sessions and assignments, while keeping tenant scope inside the
  existing RLS transaction boundary.
- Added Docker-backed read-store tests for complete, partial unscored,
  not-configured, and Reports compact score coverage signal cases.
- Extended frontend API types, view models, and route rendering so Operations
  shows a route-owned "Score coverage" monitor and Reports shows a compact
  "Score coverage" readiness signal.
- Updated route e2e fixtures/assertions to prove score coverage is visible and
  does not render sensitive fixture strings.
- Marked `OPS02` done and promoted `D73` in `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`,
  `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, `backlog.md`, and `current-roadmap.md`.
- Updated `dashboards-and-reports.md`, `campaign-lifecycle.md`, and
  `frontend-product-surfaces.md` with the aggregate-only OPS02 boundary.

**V1 reference review:** Checked read-only v1 `../AnketasProject`
`CompaniesController`, `StatisticsRepository`, `ScoreCalculatorService`,
`ResponseService`, and `ResponseRepository`. No v1 score coverage monitor
equivalent was found. Useful lesson: v1 reporting/statistics paths count
answered rows and often operate with responder/evaluation ids, so v2 should
keep this monitor response-session aggregate-only and avoid respondent ids.

**Decisions:** Coverage is response-session based, not score-row based, so
multi-dimension scores do not overcount scored submissions. Submitted responses
without any applicable scoring rule are labelled `not_configured`, not as a
scoring failure. Reports receives a compact series-level signal only; detailed
per-campaign coverage lives on Operations.

**Open questions raised:** None.

**Explicitly not done:** No raw answers, recipient emails, invitation/open-link
tokens, participant codes, IP/user-agent values, score values/documents,
per-respondent rows, batch backfill, async score workers, new scoring
operations, dashboard redesign, PDF/export infrastructure, object storage,
signed URLs, production auth/deployment, lifecycle policy, validated
interpretation, norms, thresholds, public item text, official labels, Q-047
closure, or platform-canonical instrument claims.

**Verification:**

## 2026-05-13 - codex - S08 selected-series score remediation/backfill foundation

**Goal:** Add a narrow tenant-scoped way to repair selected-series submitted
sessions that OPS02 shows as unscored despite an applicable scoring rule.

**Did:**

- Added design and implementation plan docs:
  `../../plans/2026-05-13-s08-selected-series-score-remediation-design.md` and
  `../../plans/2026-05-13-s08-selected-series-score-remediation.md`.
- Added `POST /campaign-series/{id}/score-remediation` as a tenant-member
  product-surface mutation.
- Implemented `RemediateCampaignSeriesScoresAsync` on
  `ProductSurfaceWriteStore`. It runs inside the tenant RLS transaction,
  verifies selected-series membership, loads submitted sessions through
  campaign assignments, skips already-successfully-scored sessions, and reuses
  `SubmittedResponseScoreMaterializer` with `requireScoringRule: false`.
- Preserved submit-time scoring semantics, including launch snapshot
  template/scoring-rule selection and idempotent existing-score detection.
- Returned aggregate counts only: submitted, eligible, already scored,
  remediated, skipped/not-configured, failed, and latest scoring activity.
- Added API/write-store integration coverage for route binding, aggregate
  serialization, idempotency, and cross-tenant not-found behavior.
- Added a selected-series Operations UI action under the route-owned score
  coverage monitor. The action calls remediation, shows aggregate result rows,
  and refreshes Operations coverage after completion.
- Updated roadmap, backlog, feature, architecture, and handoff docs so `S08` is
  done and `D74` is the next queue head.

recalculate/backfill, response, evaluation, and statistics equivalents. No
direct score backfill operation was found to port. Useful lesson: keep S08 out
of v1-style reporting/statistics logic and reuse v2 submit-time score
materialization.

**Decision:** `S08` closes the immediate visible-but-unrepairable score coverage
trust gap. Run `D74` next to reassess the broader product/runtime queue instead

## 2026-05-13 - codex - U08 identified respondent entry foundation

**Goal:** Add a narrow identified self-assessment respondent entry path for live
`identified` campaigns without pulling in the full respondent-rule resolver or
production auth.

**Did:**

- Added the implementation plan
  `../../plans/2026-05-13-u08-identified-respondent-entry.md`.
- Added `idn_...` operational entry tokens and
  `POST /campaigns/{id}/identified-entry` for tenant-member setup proof.
- Added public identified entry endpoints:
  `GET /respondent/identified-entries/{token}` and
  `POST /respondent/identified-entries/{token}/sessions`.
- Added `invitation_token.assignment_id` and `consent_record.subject_id` with
  EF model/migration updates plus RLS/trigger guard coverage.
- Preserved the identity invariant: identified assignments keep
  `invite_token_id = null`; report identity comes from
  `assignment.respondent_subject_id` and `consent_record.subject_id`, not from
  the entry token.
- Reused the existing public `rsh_...` response-session handle for identified
  save, draft-read, submit, URL scrubbing, and same-browser draft resilience.
- Updated `/r/[token]` to route `idn_...` credentials through the identified
  endpoints while keeping setup/admin headers out of public requests.
- Updated selected-series Operations workflow so identified campaigns show an
  "Identified entry" action, call the identified-entry setup endpoint, and skip
  anonymous invitation/delivery steps.
- Updated roadmap, backlog, feature, architecture, and handoff docs so U08 is
  done and D75 is the next queue head.

**V1 reference review:** Checked `../AnketasProject` read-only for evaluation
controllers/services/views, response service, response model, and survey
invitation model. Useful lesson: v1 known respondents are authenticated
employees and save paths reject a mismatch between persisted responder employee
and the current user claim. U08 ports the invariant, not the auth mechanism:
saves bind to the preselected identified assignment/subject and do not trust
the entry token as respondent identity.

**Decision:** U08 is enough identified-entry foundation for the current proof
spine. Run D75 next to reassess the broadest remaining product/runtime gap
before selecting deeper respondent-rule resolver, dashboard/report, export/PDF,
lifecycle, offline/PWA, production auth, or another hardening slice.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-020 still owns provider-specific production
auth/login/callback/session-store wiring.

**Explicitly not done:** No full respondent-rule resolver, subject import UI,
production login/callback/session binding, relationship target resolver,
production deliverability, individual identified reports/exports,
withdrawal/re-consent, offline sync, cross-device resume, public score
feedback, PDFs, object storage, async workers, validated interpretation, norms,
thresholds, or platform-shipped named-instrument claims.

drift, then seed a broader spec-code invariant sweep strategy.

**Did:**

- Added `Platform.SharedKernel.PlatformIds.NewId()` as the shared app-side
  persistent ID generator.
- Replaced production direct `Guid.NewGuid()` calls in infrastructure stores,
  the audit interceptor, and setup/response/scoring/report/notification code.
- Replaced the direct outbox `Guid.CreateVersion7()` call with the same shared
  helper so production has one ID creation path.
- Added `IdentifierGenerationTests` to prove UUIDv7 output and guard `src`
  against direct `Guid.NewGuid()` / `Guid.CreateVersion7()` calls outside the
  helper.
- Added
  `../../plans/2026-05-13-uuidv7-identifier-drift-and-invariant-sweep.md` as
  the seed strategy for checking similar doc-code invariant drift.
- Updated `db-schema.md`, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, and the
  roadmap so D75 includes the broader invariant pass.

**Decision:** App-created persistent IDs use UUIDv7 through
`PlatformIds.NewId()`. This slice does not add a database extension migration,
does not rewrite existing rows, and does not change the target DDL sketch that
still documents `DEFAULT uuidv7()` for database-side generated IDs.

**Open questions raised:** None.

**Explicitly not done:** No database backfill, database-side UUIDv7 extension
migration, production auth/deployment work, or broader invariant fixes beyond
the identifier policy.

**Verification:**

- Red checkpoint: filtered architecture unit tests first failed because the
  shared helper was missing and production `Guid.NewGuid()` calls existed.
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj --filter
  "IdentifierGenerationTests" --no-restore` passed.
- `dotnet build Platform.slnx -p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false
  --verbosity minimal` passed.
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj
  --filter
  "SetupEndpointTests|ResponseCaptureEndpointTests|ApplicationDbContextModelTests|RlsMigrationScriptTests"
  --no-restore` passed.

**Next:** Commit the UUIDv7 remediation. Then run D75 as the next assessment,
including the invariant sweep seed list before selecting the next executable
slice.

## 2026-05-14 - codex - OUT01 outbox payload safety boundary

**Goal:** Resolve Q-051 narrowly before worker bootstrap by enforcing a bounded
outbox JSON payload contract and preserving existing sensitive-value payload
minimization.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-14-outbox-payload-safety-boundary.md`.
- Added `OutboxPayload.MaxPayloadBytes = 64 * 1024`, serialized payload-size
  validation in `OutboxPayload.Create`, and direct `JsonDocument` validation in
  `OutboxEvent.Create`.
- Added `OutboxPayloadTests` for cap value, below-cap creation, above-cap hard
  failure, direct event validation, and separate `last_error` truncation.
- Extended the invitation-batch integration proof so queued
  `InvitationEmailQueued` outbox payloads must stay under the cap while still
  excluding raw respondent paths, raw invitation tokens, and recipient
  addresses.
- During integration verification, found a pre-existing Postgres
  `42P17 infinite recursion` failure caused by circular RLS policy references
  between `assignment` and `invitation_token`. Added migration
  `20260514090000_FixAssignmentInvitationTokenRlsRecursion` to remove the
  recursive policy references while preserving tenant/campaign policy checks
  and relying on existing FK/trigger guards for assignment-token consistency.
- Closed Q-051 in `OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, added INV-010 to
  `spec-code-invariants.md`, and updated ADR/runbook/security/schema/
  integration/roadmap/handoff docs. The active queue now points to `D77`.

**Decision:** M1 outbox event payloads have a 64 KiB UTF-8 serialized JSON hard
cap. Oversized payloads fail before persistence. Event payloads are not
silently truncated and do not use a "payload truncated" envelope. Existing
`last_error` truncation remains separate at 2,000 characters. Large future
response/session/export/PDF events must publish small ids/links and use a
future pointer pattern if they need large data.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-051 is closed for M1. Q-049 still
controls worker/outbox scheduler shape; Q-020 still controls provider-specific
production auth.

**Explicitly not done:** No Hangfire/hosted-service scheduling, concrete
dispatcher handler map, provider email delivery, payload-size metrics/alerts,
object storage, PDFs, production auth/deployment, lifecycle/withdrawal policy,
full respondent-rule resolver depth, captured-log sentinel tests, or
platform-shipped named-instrument content.

**Verification:**

- Red checkpoint:
  `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj --filter
  "OutboxPayloadTests" --no-restore` failed as expected because
  `OutboxPayload.MaxPayloadBytes` did not exist.
- Debug checkpoint:
  `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'; dotnet test
  tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter
  "Campaign_invitation_batch_store_creates_email_tokens_assignments_notifications_and_outbox_intents"
  --no-restore` first failed with Postgres `42P17: infinite recursion detected
  in policy for relation "assignment"`.
- Green unit checkpoint:
  `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj --filter
  "OutboxPayloadTests|SpecCodeInvariantTests" --no-restore` passed: 8 passed,
  0 failed.
- Green integration checkpoint:
  `$env:RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS='1'; dotnet test
  tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter
  "Campaign_invitation_batch_store_creates_email_tokens_assignments_notifications_and_outbox_intents"
  --no-restore` passed: 1 passed, 0 failed.
- `dotnet build Platform.slnx -p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false
  --verbosity minimal` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
- `git diff --check` passed with only line-ending normalization warnings.
- Active queue scans confirmed no stale active OUT01 references and current
  queue references point to `D77`.

**Next:** Commit OUT01. Then run `D77` post-OUT01 outbox/runtime assessment
unless the owner changes priority.

## 2026-05-14 - codex - OUT02 outbox dispatcher contract foundation

**Goal:** Make in-process outbox dispatch semantics explicit before any relay
scheduler/bootstrap slice can mark rows published.

**Did:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-14-outbox-dispatcher-contract.md`.
- Added `IOutboxEventHandler`, `OutboxEventDispatcher`,
  `OutboxEventHandlerNotFoundException`, `AddOutboxDispatching()`, and
  `InvitationEmailQueuedOutboxHandler` under `src/Platform.Workers/Outbox`.
- Added dispatcher contract tests for registered routing, unknown-handler
  failure, duplicate registration rejection, known M1 handler registration, and
  the explicit `InvitationEmailQueued` no-op handler.
- Added a Docker-backed relay proof that an unknown event type remains
  unpublished and moves to retry instead of being marked published.
- Updated ADR-0009, `spec-code-invariants.md`, `runbook.md`,
  `OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, and
  `current-roadmap.md`.

**Decision:** Unknown or unregistered outbox event types are dispatch failures,
not success. The relay catches those failures and keeps the row unpublished for
retry/dead-letter handling. `InvitationEmailQueued` is the only known M1
handler registered by OUT02, and it is an explicit no-op acknowledgement of the
existing minimized outbox intent. Provider-safe email delivery remains the
separate notification delivery path.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-049 still controls relay
scheduler/bootstrap shape.

**Explicitly not done:** No Hangfire/hosted-service scheduling, provider email
delivery, real webhooks, MassTransit/RabbitMQ, observability plumbing, payload
rejection metrics/alerts, auth, PDFs/object storage, lifecycle policy, full
respondent-rule resolver depth, captured-log sentinels, or platform-shipped
named-instrument content.

## 2026-05-14 - codex - OUT03 outbox relay scheduler/bootstrap foundation

**Goal:** Add the M1 outbox relay scheduler/bootstrap foundation so due
outbox messages have a real worker-host execution path.

**Did:**

- Added design and execution plans for OUT03.
- Performed a read-only v1 reference review of hosted background services in
  `..\AnketasProject`; ported the scoped-service-per-tick lesson and avoided
  v1's timer/`async void` pattern.
- Added `Platform.Workers` host bootstrap with application and infrastructure
  registration, outbox dispatching, and worker appsettings.
- Added the outbox relay hosted service, options, tick processor abstraction,
  and service registration.
- Added focused worker bootstrap tests covering DI registration, configured
  batch size, disabled mode, tick failure isolation, and option validation.
- Added a narrow license-policy review entry for the new transitive
  `System.Memory` dependency pulled by `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting`; local
  package metadata uses the legacy CoreFX `licenseUrl`, and package
  `LICENSE.TXT` is MIT.
- Updated ADR, architecture, runbook, invariant ledger, open-question, roadmap,
  next-action, next-session, and README documentation.

**Decision:** Use the standard .NET `BackgroundService` foundation for M1
instead of adding Hangfire now. Hangfire remains a reviewed candidate, but its
current package/license posture is conditional under the repo dependency policy,
so Q-049 stays open for future owner/legal review, scaling pressure, or M2
broker topology.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-049 remains open only for the later
scheduler/scaling/broker decision; it no longer blocks the M1 hosted-service
foundation.

**Explicitly not done:** No provider SMTP/email delivery, real webhooks,

deployment/health checks, auth, PDFs/object storage, lifecycle/retention
automation, full respondent-rule resolver depth, captured-log sentinels, or

## 2026-05-14 - codex - LOG01 captured-log sensitive-value sentinel foundation

**Goal:** Add a captured-log/failure-metadata sentinel foundation and sanitize
the current worker/outbox diagnostic leaks.

**Did:**

- Added LOG01 design and implementation plans.
- Performed read-only v1 reference review in `..\AnketasProject`; found no
  safe sentinel pattern and found v1 examples that log exception messages and
  password-reset token prefixes, so LOG01 intentionally did not port that
  pattern.
- Added test-only captured logging support under
  `tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Support/Logging`.
- Added sentinel assertions for raw participant codes, invitation/open-link/
  identified-entry tokens, public response-session handles, raw answer text,
  connection strings, provider credentials, salts, and raw respondent paths.
- Sanitized `OutboxRelayWorker` tick failure logging so it logs exception type
  without passing raw exception objects/messages to `ILogger`.
- Sanitized `OutboxRelay` failure metadata so `outbox_event.last_error` stores
  `DISPATCH_FAILED` with event type, aggregate type, and exception type instead
  of raw exception messages.
- Added a captured-log guard for current public respondent boundary failure
  paths.
- Updated invariant, runbook, roadmap, next-action, and next-session handoff
  docs.

**Decision:** Keep LOG01 source-level and test-focused. Do not add a production
redaction registry or OpenTelemetry stack until a later observability slice.

production auth, Q-049 still owns future Hangfire/scaling/M2 broker revisit,

rejection observability, API/worker health endpoint hardening, or
platform-shipped named-instrument content.

## 2026-05-14 - AU02 provider-neutral auth configuration readiness guard

  when OIDC authority/audience are missing or blank, HTTPS metadata is
  disabled, or authority is not absolute HTTPS.

  audience, provider, client, secret, token, metadata, exception, or raw config
  details.

  does not throw before health probes can report unready; auth remains unable to
  validate successfully without safe provider configuration.

  action, and next-session docs.

## 2026-05-14 - VAL02 validation demo tenant source checks and seed specification

  Danijel Kucak / Algebra, and Croatian OSH framing.

**Decision:** VAL03 may seed proof-only validation tenants, but must use the
new source gate:

- CBI exact English can be used for private proof with source/provenance and
  non-canonical labels.
- COPSOQ III exact text can be used only when an official unmodified source
  version is selected and COPSOQ license/guideline conditions are followed.
- NMQ exact item text stays out; use custom NMQ-style ergonomics/MSK wording.
- NOSACQ-50 is an optional exact OSH safety-climate source; a shorter
  synthetic pulse is acceptable for demo speed.
- Danijel/Algebra education and Croatian `zastita na radu` workflows use
  synthetic custom content by default.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-021 final production hosting, Q-053
production legal package, O02-B, and O03 real consultant outreach remain open.

**Explicitly not done:** No seed implementation, exact item text, real
participant/student/employee data, provider auth code, production deployment,
legal advice, GDPR/DPA readiness claim, MBI, OLBI, or platform-canonical
named-instrument content was added.

## 2026-05-14 - codex - AUTH01 Auth0 Free login/session foundation

**Goal:** Add the first provider-specific Auth0 Free login/callback/logout and
app-session foundation without moving tenant authorization into Auth0.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`, `OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`,
ADR-0011, `auth-and-authz.md`, deployment/security docs, current roadmap, VAL03
seed docs, and read-only v1 auth references in `../AnketasProject`.

**V1 reference review:** V1 had no Auth0/OIDC provider flow to port. The useful
lesson was the AdminPortal pattern: after login, create a server-owned cookie
and project application roles/permissions from the database into claims. V1 also
handled API/AJAX unauthorized/forbidden responses as status codes instead of
HTML login redirects. AUTH01 ported those lessons, not v1 local-password/JWT
code.

**Work completed:**

- Added `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.OpenIdConnect` and opt-in
  interactive OIDC mode under `Authentication:Oidc:InteractiveEnabled=true`.
- Added app-cookie and OIDC scheme constants, hardened cookie defaults
  (`HttpOnly`, `Secure`, `SameSite=Lax`), API 401/403 redirect handling,
  OpenID Connect code+PKCE configuration, `/auth/login`, and `/auth/logout`.
- Added OIDC token-validation projection through `PlatformOidcEvents`: verified
  email required by default, login tenant id required, platform login resolved
  through tenant-scoped DB lookup, and only `user_id`, `tenant_memberships`, and
  `permissions` platform claims are written into the app cookie.
- Kept Auth0 as identity provider only. Auth0 Organizations/roles/SCIM are not
  used as platform tenant or authorization source.
- Updated `/health/ready` and `/health/startup` OIDC readiness so interactive
  mode accepts authority + client id/secret instead of requiring JWT audience.
- Updated Svelte API client to send credentials by default, added
  `PUBLIC_TENANT_ID` session headers, and derived login URLs with `tenantId`.
- Updated VPS staging example to disable development auth and carry Auth0
  placeholders for Hostinger demo/staging validation.
- Added AUTH01 design and implementation plan under `docs/plans/`.

**Verification run during slice:**

- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "AuthEndpointTests" --no-restore`
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "HealthEndpointTests.Ready_health_reports_oidc_configuration_ok_for_interactive_auth0_configuration" --no-restore`

- `node --test tests/deployment-package/local-staging-package.test.mjs`

**Decision:** AUTH01 is an M1/demo-staging foundation. Provider-subject binding,
refresh-token rotation/session store, full anti-forgery token wiring for
cookie-authenticated mutations, SSO/SCIM, paid Auth0, production hosting,
legal/GDPR readiness, real-person data, and user-management UX remain out of
scope.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-021 final production hosting and Q-053
production legal/entity/GDPR path remain open. Q-020 remains closed for M1 by
ADR-0011.

**Next:** OB03 final Q-021 hosting decision ADR/cost refresh before any
real-person data, paid-pilot, hospital/public-sector, or production-hosting
claim.

## 2026-05-14 - codex - OB03 portable Docker hosting posture

**Goal:** Close Q-021 into a concrete M1 hosting posture without provisioning
production infrastructure or weakening the Q-053 real-person-data gate.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`,
`OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, ADR-0011, deployment/security/runbook/observability/cost
docs, current roadmap, owner-blocker packet, validation demo tenant docs, and
read-only v1 hosting/deployment references in `../AnketasProject`.

**V1 reference review:** V1 already used Docker Compose on the VPS with
loopback-bound app ports, `.env`-driven configuration, and SSH-based rebuild
automation. OB03 ported the lesson that a plain VPS + Compose is viable for
early validation, but v2 needs a clearer portable contract, backup/restore gate,
and no hidden coupling to one host. No v1 code or deployment files were
modified.

**Work completed:**

- Added accepted `../70-decisions/0012-m1-portable-docker-hosting-posture.md`.

## 2026-05-14 - codex - AUTH02 provider-subject binding and session hardening

**Goal:** Harden the Auth0/OIDC interactive session path before any real user
cohort, while keeping Auth0 as identity provider only and Q-053 blocked for
real-person data.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`,
`OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, ADR-0011, ADR-0012, `auth-and-authz.md`, `security.md`,
`q053-counsel-ready-legal-packet.md`, `auth0.md`, the current roadmap, and the
AUTH02 implementation plan.

**V1 reference review:** V1 had no Auth0/OIDC provider-subject binding or
local OIDC session store to port. AUTH02 used the prior v1 lesson already
captured in AUTH01: login should end in a server-owned app session whose
authorization claims come from the platform database, not the identity
provider.

**Work completed:**

- Added tenant-scoped `external_auth_identity` and `auth_session` persistence,
  EF mappings, migration, and RLS coverage.
- Added deterministic provider-subject hashing so raw provider `sub` values
  are not persisted.
- Changed OIDC login resolution so verified email is only the bootstrap path:
  first login binds provider + subject hash, later logins must match the
  existing binding, disabled bindings fail closed, and provider-subject hashes
  cannot bind to two users in the same tenant.
- Issued local `auth_session` rows during OIDC login and projected `session_id`
  into the app cookie.
- Added app-cookie validation against the active local session, expected user
  and tenant membership, expiry/revocation state, and enabled external identity.
- Revoked the current local session on logout with reason `logout`.
- Added `/auth/csrf` and middleware that requires `X-CSRF-TOKEN` for unsafe
  cookie-authenticated tenant-member mutations while skipping public respondent
  routes and development test-auth requests.
- Added opt-in Svelte API-client CSRF support for authenticated app clients;
  public respondent clients remain unchanged.
- Updated `auth-and-authz.md`, `security.md`,
  `q053-counsel-ready-legal-packet.md`, `auth0.md`, `current-roadmap.md`,
  `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, and `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`.

**Decision:** AUTH02 closes the local provider-subject/session/CSRF hardening
slice for M1 demo/staging use. Auth0 remains identity provider only. The app
continues to own tenants, users, roles, permissions, local sessions, CSRF, and
authorization.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-053 remains open and still blocks real
participant/student/employee/patient data, paid production pilots, final
DPA/privacy/GDPR claims, legal entity setup, and production launch. Q-049
still controls future worker/outbox scaling and any Hangfire revisit.

**Explicitly not done:** No full refresh-token rotation or token-family reuse
detection, Auth0 Organizations/roles as platform authorization, paid Auth0,
SSO/SCIM, broad user-management UX, live VPS deployment, backup/restore proof,
production monitoring provider, legal/GDPR readiness claim, or real-person
data approval was added.

  creates a PostgreSQL custom-format `pg_dump` under ignored
  `artifacts/deployment-dr/backups`, starts a disposable restore Postgres
  service under a separate Compose project, runs `pg_restore`, verifies public
  schema/table presence and required platform tables, and removes the restore
  volume by default.

  pass/fail status; it does not print row data, participant codes, invitation
  tokens, salts, raw answers, connection strings, or secret values.

  non-sensitive output posture, and DR documentation references.

  `vps-staging-runbook.md`, `deployment.md`, `current-roadmap.md`,
  `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, and `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`.

**Decision:** DEPLOY-DR01 is closed as a manual, owner-run smoke for validating
that the current portable Compose database can be dumped and restored. It is a
host trust proof for demo/staging validation, not a production DR program.

participant/student/employee/patient data, paid production pilots, final

## 2026-05-14 - codex - Auth0 local staging browser smoke fix

**Goal:** Unblock the owner-run Auth0 local staging smoke after verified Auth0
login reached the platform but `/app` showed `Session check failed` /
`Failed to fetch`.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`,
`OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, AUTH01/AUTH02 auth endpoints, tenant middleware,
development-auth CORS tests, VPS staging runbook, deployment docs, and staging
Compose env examples.

**Root cause:** Auth0-backed staging runs the web app and API on different
origins (`5174` -> `5055` locally; web/API subdomains on VPS). CORS was only
enabled when Development auth was enabled, so browser preflight for
`/auth/session` with `X-Tenant-Id` failed with `405`. A second adjacent gap was
that Auth0 callback could only return to local API paths such as `/app`, leaving
the browser on the API origin instead of the web origin.

**Work completed:**

- Added configured browser CORS support outside Development auth via
  `Cors:AllowedOrigins`.
- Kept Development auth fallback CORS behavior for local dev.
- Allowed Auth0 login/logout `returnUrl` values to be absolute only when their
  origin exactly matches a configured browser CORS origin; arbitrary external
  return URLs remain rejected.
- Updated VPS staging env/compose docs so Auth0 staging requires explicit web
  CORS origin and API auth URLs with web-origin return URLs.
- Updated ignored local `deploy/staging/.env` for the owner smoke: CORS origin
  is the local web origin, and login/logout return to the local web app.
- Rebuilt the local Auth0-mode Compose stack and left API/web/Postgres running.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-053 still blocks real participant,
student, employee, patient, paid-tenant, DPA/privacy/GDPR, and production legal
claims.

**Explicitly not done:** No Auth0 users, Auth0 Management API, Auth0
Organizations/roles, paid Auth0, user-management UX, real-person data approval,
VPS mutation, GitHub Actions, production legal/GDPR claim, or secret-bearing
docs were added.

**Verification run during slice:**

- Red CORS regression first failed with `405 MethodNotAllowed`.
- Red allowed-web-return regression first failed with `400 BadRequest`.
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Platform.IntegrationTests.Api.AuthEndpointTests|Development_cors_allows_configured_local_frontend_origin|Configured_cors_allows_auth0_mode_frontend_origin_outside_development_auth" --no-restore --verbosity minimal` passed: 35/35.
- `node --test tests/deployment-package/local-staging-package.test.mjs` passed:
  14/14.
- `git diff --check` passed; Git printed only LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings.
- Rebuilt local Auth0-mode staging stack with Compose and confirmed live
  preflight for `/auth/session` from `http://127.0.0.1:5174` returns `204` with
  credentialed CORS headers.
- Confirmed local login endpoint with allowed web-origin return URL returns
  `302 Found`, not `400`, and the local web `/app` route is served with the
  configured API-return login href.

**Next:** Owner can retry `http://127.0.0.1:5174/app` in the browser. If the
existing browser session expired, sign in again from the app; the flow should
return to the web app instead of the API `/app` 404. After smoke, return to D83.

**Follow-up during owner smoke:** Browser still reported CORS because
unauthenticated `/auth/session` was using the OIDC challenge scheme and
redirecting the XHR to Auth0. Changed interactive auth's default challenge back
to the app-cookie scheme so API/session checks return `401` with CORS instead
of provider redirects; `/auth/login` still explicitly challenges Auth0 for the
full-page sign-in flow. Added regression
`Session_check_returns_unauthorized_instead_of_oidc_redirect_when_cookie_missing`.
Rebuilt local Auth0-mode staging and confirmed live `/auth/session` returns
`401 Unauthorized` with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://127.0.0.1:5174`.

**Second follow-up during owner smoke:** Declining the Auth0 consent prompt
surfaced as callback/provider errors because OIDC remote failures were not
handled. Added a remote-failure handler that redirects back to the already
validated app return URL with `auth=failed`, preserving the existing external
return-url allowlist. Added optional `/auth/login?...&prompt=login` support for
manual smoke so the owner can force Auth0 to show a login screen instead of
reusing SSO. Updated ignored local `.env` to include `prompt=login`, rebuilt
local Auth0-mode staging, and confirmed the web login href and provider
authorize redirect include `prompt=login`.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - D86 production-like demo readiness assessment

**Goal:** Assess the next concrete production-like demo slice after VAL07 and

  `q053-counsel-ready-legal-packet.md`, and
  `OWNER-BLOCKERS-ACTION-PACK.md`: Q-053 blocks real-person data, paid
  production use, and tenant-facing GDPR/DPA/legal claims, but does not block
  production-grade engineering with synthetic, seed, demo, or owner-controlled
  test data.

**Decision:** Select `RBAC01` - role-aware product access foundation. Current
Auth0 sessions and validation role slots already carry app-owned permissions,
but many setup/product/report mutation endpoints still authorize only tenant
membership and the frontend does not consistently hide owner/researcher
controls for analyst/viewer sessions. RBAC01 should use existing
`setup.manage` as the first narrow capability boundary: safe reads remain
tenant-member; unsafe setup, launch, archive/restore, close, score remediation,
delivery, report/export, lab response/scoring, and export download paths
require permission and get frontend read-only states.

gate for real-person data and production legal/GDPR claims.

Auth0 Management API, paid Auth0, broad user-management UX, in-app tenant
switching, live VPS mutation, deployment automation, dashboard redesign,
PDF/object storage, real-person data approval, or legal/GDPR claim was added.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - RBAC01 role-aware product access foundation

**Goal:** Make the existing app-owned `setup.manage` permission authoritative
for owner/researcher actions while preserving safe read access for
analyst/viewer sessions.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`,
`OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, D86 assessment, current roadmap, `auth-and-authz.md`,
ADR-0011, Q-053 counsel packet, and current setup/product/report/response/
scoring endpoint authorization patterns. V1 read-only reference review checked
`../AnketasProject` role/permission constants, JWT claim projection, role-gated
partials, and evaluation-flow denial tests. Useful lesson ported: permissions
belong in app-owned claims, and non-manager/non-owner denial needs explicit
tests.

**Work completed:**

- Added `docs/plans/2026-05-15-rbac01-role-aware-product-access.md`.
- Added `PlatformPermissions.SetupManage`.
- Required both tenant membership and `setup.manage` on unsafe setup,
  product-surface mutation, report/export, export artifact retrieval/download,
  notification delivery processing, tenant lab response write, and scoring
  endpoints.
- Left safe read endpoints tenant-member only, including instruments,
  campaign-series reads, setup/operations/reports/waves read models, report
  proof, wave comparison proof, and respondent campaign reads.
- Added integration tests proving tenant members without `setup.manage` can
  read safe surfaces but receive `403 Forbidden` on unsafe actions.
- Added product auth context in the Svelte app shell and consumed session
  permissions across product surfaces.
- Portfolio create/rename/archive/restore controls and selected-series setup,
  operations, reports, score remediation, and default proof-workbench actions
  are hidden/read-only when `setup.manage` is absent.
- Added Playwright coverage for read-only product surfaces and refreshed the
  product-surface harness with a CSRF token mock for unsafe app-client actions.

**Decision:** `setup.manage` is the narrow first product capability. It keeps
the owner/researcher action set credible for validation demos and makes
analyst/viewer sessions read-only without adding role-specific Auth0 plumbing
or a broader permission taxonomy. Export retrieval/download stays behind
`setup.manage` until export-specific permissions are designed.

**Open questions raised:** None new. Q-053 remains a launch/legal-use gate for
real-person data and production legal/GDPR claims, not a blocker for
synthetic/demo engineering.

**Explicitly not done:** No Auth0 Organizations/roles, Auth0 Management API,
paid Auth0 feature, broad user-management UX, tenant switching, deployment
automation, dashboard redesign, real-person data approval, or legal/GDPR claim
was added.

**Verification run during slice:**

- Red targeted integration run failed first on the new forbidden assertions,
  proving unsafe endpoints were still tenant-member only.
- `dotnet test tests\Platform.IntegrationTests\Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SetupEndpointTests|FullyQualifiedName~ProductSurfaceEndpointTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReportProofEndpointTests|FullyQualifiedName~ResponseCaptureEndpointTests|FullyQualifiedName~ScoringEndpointTests" -p:UseSharedCompilation=false -m:1 -nr:false --verbosity minimal` passed: 75/75.
- `node node_modules\@sveltejs\kit\svelte-kit.js sync; node node_modules\vite\bin\vite.js build` passed.
- Focused read-only Playwright test passed: 1/1.
- Full `product-surfaces.e2e.ts` passed: 46/46.
- `svelte-check` was attempted through direct node invocation; it still reports
  existing `src/lib/api/client.test.ts` tuple/mock typing errors unrelated to
  this slice.
- Final `git diff --check` passed; Git printed only LF-to-CRLF working-copy
  warnings.
- Added-line secret scan returned only the intentional Playwright
  `test-csrf-token` fixture string and CSRF documentation wording.

**Next:** Run `D87` post-RBAC01 production-like demo assessment unless the

## 2026-05-15 - codex - D87 post-RBAC01 production-like demo assessment

**Goal:** Reassess the next production-like demo limiter after RBAC01 made

the existing proof-workflow test suite can still exercise it outside the normal

## 2026-05-15 - codex - TEAM02 tenant member invite and role assignment foundation

**Goal:** Add the first app-owned tenant team management write path without
turning Auth0 into the platform authorization source.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`,
`OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, current roadmap, D93 assessment,
TEAM02 design and implementation plans, auth/Auth0 docs, TEAM01 roster code,
ProductSurfaces endpoints/read stores/write stores, auth session persistence,
validation auth bootstrap, deployment package tests, and `/app/team`.

**V1 reference review:** Read-only review of sibling
`../AnketasProject` focused on `BusinessLayer/Features/RoleManagement` and
company setup role assignment. V1 has employee-role add/remove and company
default/user/manager role procedures, but no provider-backed member-preparation
model to port. Useful lesson retained: keep role assignment app-owned and
explicit; v2 also revokes target local app sessions on role change.

**Did:**

- Added app permission `team.manage` and ProductSurfaces routes:
  `GET /tenant-roles`, `POST /tenant-members`, and
  `PUT /tenant-members/{userId}/tenant-role`.
- Added read-store support for assignable current-tenant roles and active vs
  pending provider-link status on tenant members.
- Added write-store support to prepare a tenant member by normalized verified
  email/role/locale, create the app-side `user_account` when missing, assign a
  tenant role, change another member's primary tenant role, preserve
  resource-scope assignments, reject self role changes, and revoke active local
  app sessions for the changed member.
- Updated `/auth/session` coverage so `team.manage` flows into session
  permissions.
- Updated validation auth bootstrap so owner slots get `setup.manage` and
  `team.manage`, while researcher slots stay setup-only.
- Updated `/app/team` with manager-only add-member and role-change controls,
  assignable role loading, provider-link status badges, and read-only
  analyst/viewer behavior.
- Updated frontend API types/client methods and product-surface browser tests.
- Updated walkthrough packet, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, and
  current roadmap so TEAM02 is done and D94 is next.

**Decisions:** Auth0 remains identity provider only. Member preparation creates
or updates platform-side app access for a verified email; it does not create an
Auth0 user, send email, manage Auth0 organizations/roles, call Auth0 Management
API, or claim production legal/GDPR readiness. The current role taxonomy remains
seed/demo-grade and should be reassessed through D94 before broadening.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-053 remains a real-person/legal-claims gate
only; it does not block synthetic/demo engineering.

**Explicitly not done:** No Auth0 Management API, Auth0 Organizations/roles,
SCIM, paid Auth0 dependency, real email delivery, member hard delete, final
role taxonomy, VPS deployment/mutation, GitHub Actions, real-person data
approval, production legal/GDPR claim, landing page, or platform-canonical
named-instrument content was added.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - ORG01 subject directory and hierarchy foundation

**Goal:** Add the first normal product surface for tenant subjects, groups,
memberships, and manager relationships.

**Context read:** `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, latest `SESSION-LOG.md`,
`OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`, current roadmap, D94 assessment, ORG01 design and
implementation plans, subject domain objects, ProductSurfaces endpoint/store
patterns, `/app/team`, product API client tests, validation demo seed scripts,
and respondent-rule/domain hierarchy docs.

**V1 reference review:** Read-only review of `../AnketasProject` covered
`HierarchyBuilder`, evaluation-flow tests, seed service interfaces, and response
service interfaces. V1 uses `Employee.SupervisorId` plus organizational units,
and its tests distinguish ordinary user, manager, target, peers, and manager
360 flows. Lesson retained: keep manager relationship direction explicit and
separate hierarchy role from app authorization role. ORG01 only creates graph
data; later respondent-rule work should consume it deliberately.

**Did:**

- Added ProductSurfaces contracts, handlers, endpoint routes, read-store and
  write-store methods for `GET/POST/PUT /subjects`,
  `GET/POST /subject-groups`, `POST /subject-groups/{groupId}/members`, and
  `PUT /subjects/{subjectId}/manager`.
- Added domain support for updating subject directory profile fields and ending
  active subject relationships.
- Added tenant-scoped directory projections with manager name, direct-report
  count, group memberships, group member counts, and setup-manager-only
  authorization.
- Added integration/unit coverage for missing setup permission, directory
  projection, subject/group validation, wrong-tenant membership rejection,
  canonical `manager_of` replacement/clearing, and relationship ending.
- Added frontend product API types/methods/tests for all ORG01 endpoints.
- Added `/app/directory` and navigation with subject/group creation,
  selected-subject editing, group membership binding, manager setting/clearing,
  summary metrics, loading/error/empty states, and directory/group read models.
- Extended validation demo fixtures and `seed-validation-demo.ps1` so OH,
  education, and OSH tenants seed synthetic subjects, groups, memberships, and
  manager links through the same product APIs. Duplicate-seed checks now include
  subject, subject group, and subject relationship rows.
- Updated `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, current roadmap, and
  session log.

**Decisions:** ORG01 uses setup-level authorization for both read and write
directory access. The canonical manager edge remains `manager_of` from manager
subject to employee/target subject. Validation hierarchy uses fictional
synthetic people and is tenant-scoped demo data only.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-053 remains a real-person/legal-claims gate
only; it does not block synthetic/demo engineering.

**Explicitly not done:** No full respondent-rule resolver, immutable assignment
generation, reseeding, invitation creation from rules, bulk import/export, org
chart visualization, broad UI component system, final directory role taxonomy,
real-person data approval, production legal/GDPR claim, or
platform-canonical named-instrument content was added.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - RR01 respondent-rule audience preview foundation

**Goal:** Add the first normal-product respondent-rule preview surface so setup
managers can inspect how a narrow rule resolves against tenant subjects, groups,
memberships, and manager relationships before launch.

**Context read:** Root/v2 AGENTS, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`,
latest session log entries, current roadmap, RR01 assessment state, product
surface contracts/endpoints/stores, setup workflow UI, product API tests, and
the ORG01 directory model.

**V1 reference review:** Read-only review of the sibling `AnketasProject`
checked `BusinessLayer/Utilities/HierarchyBuilder.cs`,
`AdminPortal/Helpers/OrganizationEmployeeNodeBuilder.cs`, and
`BusinessLayer/Helpers/StoredProcedureCommands.cs`. V1 models hierarchy through
`SupervisorId`, builds subordinate trees from manager/supervisor direction, and
has target/responder stored-procedure surfaces for evaluation reporting. RR01
ported the lesson, not code: keep `manager_of` direction explicit as manager
source -> target/report, and keep preview read-only instead of silently creating
launch assignments.

**Did:**

- Added `POST /campaign-series/{seriesId}/campaigns/{campaignId}/respondent-rule-preview`
  behind tenant context, tenant membership, and `setup.manage`.
- Added request/response contracts, MediatR query/validator/handler, product
  API client method, and setup workflow UI for previewing supported rule kinds.
- Implemented read-store resolution for `self`, `all_in_group`,
  `manager_of_target`, and `reports_of_target` with tenant fail-closed campaign,
  group, subject, membership, and relationship checks.
- Added warnings for missing campaign audience fallback, empty group, missing
  manager/report relationships, empty results, row truncation, and validation
  errors for unsupported/malformed rules or missing selectors.
- Added endpoint, read-store, frontend API, and product-surfaces E2E coverage
  for preview binding, authorization, cross-tenant rejection, selector
  validation, hierarchy direction, and setup UI request/response rendering.
- Updated current roadmap, `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, and `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md` so
  RR01 is done and D96 is the next assessment.

**Decisions:** RR01 is preview-only and deliberately narrow. It does not create
assignments, invitations, launch snapshots, reseeding behavior, full 360 logic,
bulk import/export, org chart visualization, or a generic UI widget system.
`self` previews campaign audience when present, then falls back to active tenant
subjects with an explicit warning so empty draft campaigns remain inspectable.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-053 remains a real-person/legal-claims gate
only; it does not block synthetic/demo engineering.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - Fix local Auth0 Docker CSRF failure

**Goal:** Diagnose owner-reported 500s in local Docker Auth0 mode after the
RR02 merge.

**Did:**

- Reproduced the backend failure from Docker logs: `/auth/csrf` threw because
  antiforgery cookies used production-only `SecurePolicy = Always` while the
  local Docker inspection stack runs plain HTTP.
- Added a regression test proving authenticated tenant members can fetch CSRF
  tokens over local HTTP in Development.
- Changed only Development antiforgery cookie settings to use a non-`__Host-`
  local cookie name and `SameAsRequest`; production keeps `__Host-...` and
  `SecurePolicy = Always`.
- Rebuilt/restarted the local Docker staging API/web stack.

**Decisions:** This is a local Development HTTP allowance only. Production
cookie posture remains hardened.

**Open questions raised:** None.

**Verification run during slice:**

- The new CSRF local-HTTP regression failed red with HTTP 500 before the fix.
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Csrf_endpoint_returns_token_for_tenant_member_over_local_http_in_development|FullyQualifiedName~Csrf_endpoint_returns_token_for_tenant_member|FullyQualifiedName~Interactive_oidc_cookie_is_hardened_for_browser_session" --logger "console;verbosity=minimal"`
  passed: 3/3 after the fix.
- `dotnet test tests/Platform.IntegrationTests/Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~AuthEndpointTests|FullyQualifiedName~SetupEndpointTests" --logger "console;verbosity=minimal"`
  passed: 71/71.
- `deploy/staging/start-local-staging.ps1` rebuilt and restarted the local
  Docker staging stack.
- Docker status showed Postgres healthy and API/web running on `5055` and
  `5174`.
- `GET http://127.0.0.1:5055/health` returned `ok`.
- `GET http://127.0.0.1:5174/` returned HTTP 200 and the configured tenant sign
  in link.
- Fresh API logs after restart showed no antiforgery exception.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - Fix local Docker runtime DB role for RLS

**Goal:** Diagnose owner-reported `Campaign configuration` 404 after the RR02
merge and local Docker refresh.

**Did:**

- Proved the local Docker API was using the initial Postgres `platform_app`
  role, which is a superuser in the Compose database and bypasses RLS.
- Confirmed that product-surface campaign-series queries could therefore expose
  validation campaigns from other tenants, while RR02 campaign-configuration
  endpoints correctly returned 404 for those wrong-tenant campaign IDs.
- Added `deploy/staging/runtime-role.sql` and a one-shot `runtime-role` Compose
  service to create/grant a least-privilege `platform_app_runtime` role.
- Switched the API Compose connection string to `platform_app_runtime`; migrator
  and seed still use the admin role.
- Rebuilt/restarted local Docker staging without wiping the Postgres volume.
- Updated local/VPS env examples, deployment package tests, and the VPS staging
  runbook to document the runtime role.

## 2026-05-15 - codex - WGT01 reports widget manifest foundation

**Goal:** Add the first backend-served widget manifest to the Reports surface
without changing existing report semantics or turning this into a full
dashboard builder.

**Did:**

- Added `GET /campaign-series/{id}/reports-widget-manifest`, typed manifest
  contracts, a ProductSurfaces query, route wiring, and a read-store resolver
  over the existing selected-series Reports workspace.
- Emitted versioned aggregate/report-safe widgets for readiness, score
  coverage, selected-campaign report state, export artifact registry, visual
  analytics entry, and finality provenance.
- Filtered report-proof and export actions by actor permissions and
  finality-aware reportability; response-export download capability remains
  available only to setup managers when the artifact is downloadable.
- Added frontend API/types, data guards, a local Svelte widget registry,
  supported readiness/score-coverage widgets, an unsupported-widget fallback,
  and a Reports widget section before the existing Reports workflow.
- Kept the manifest request best-effort so delay/failure does not block the
  existing Reports route panels and actions.
- Updated `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, `current-roadmap.md`, and
  `backlog.md` so `D98` is next.

**Decisions:** WGT01 establishes the widget-manifest pattern on the safest
aggregate surface first. It does not add a full dashboard builder, arbitrary
plugin loading, tenant-authored layouts, new report math, PDFs, raw export
depth, Designer work, real-person data approval, legal/GDPR claims, or
platform-canonical named-instrument content.

only; it does not block production-grade engineering with synthetic, seed,
demo, or owner-controlled test data.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - UX26 product wayfinding and workspace focus foundation

**Goal:** Add a shared selected-series workspace frame/navigation and clearer
primary-vs-reference composition across selected-series routes without changing
backend semantics.

**V1 reference review:** Checked read-only v1 screens in
`..\..\..\AnketasProject`, including `UserPortal` home/evaluation-statistics
views and `AdminPortal` company evaluation/group-statistics views. Useful
lesson: v1 keeps the current evaluation/company context, tabs/breadcrumb-like
navigation, and current filter state near the top before tables/actions. UX26
ports that product lesson as selected-series workspace identity/navigation
before route-owned work, without copying v1 code.

**Did:**

- Added a failing Playwright wayfinding contract that covers hub, Setup,
  Operations, Reports, and Waves through the `Selected series workspace` group.
- Added `SelectedSeriesWorkspaceNav.svelte` and compact workspace/reference CSS.
- Wired selected-series hub through the shared workspace frame, consolidated
  hub child-route links into that nav, and removed repeated visible series UUID
  details from the hub reference rows.
- Wired Setup, Operations, Reports, and Waves through the shared selected-series
  workspace frame before each route's primary work/result region.
- Preserved UX25 primary-before-reference ordering by keeping dense context
  inside explicit `reference-context` regions.
- Kept backend APIs, report math, widget manifest semantics, Designer,
  deployment, legal/GDPR claims, and platform-canonical named-instrument
  content out of scope.

**Decisions:** UX26 is a frontend wayfinding/focus foundation, not another broad
visual redesign. The shared workspace owns selected-series identity, child-route
navigation, active-route state, and posture labels; route components still own
their workflows, snapshots, dashboards, and reference context.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-053 remains a real-person/legal-use gate
only; it does not block production-grade engineering with synthetic, seed,
demo, or owner-controlled test data.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - UX27 product language and trust vocabulary cleanup

**Goal:** Make normal authenticated app workflow copy read like product copy
instead of an internal proof harness while preserving truth/provenance labels.

**Did:**

- Added a Playwright normal-app vocabulary contract for `/app`,
  `/app/campaign-series`, selected-series hub, Setup, Operations, Reports, and
  Waves. The contract bans proof-harness workflow phrases while allowing
  Proof/local, proof-only, and not-validated provenance labels.
- Cleaned selected-series view-model copy from proof-action wording to setup
  actions, operations actions, report preview actions, and wave comparison
  actions.
- Cleaned Operations workflow copy from proof entry/local delivery proof wording
  to entry link, identified entry token, invitation batch, and local delivery.
- Cleaned Reports workflow, route, snapshot, and widget copy to report preview,
  export artifact, response export, stored artifact, and CSV download language.
- Cleaned Waves workflow, route, and snapshot copy to linked trajectory check
  and wave comparison preview language.
- Updated demo fixture copy and the validation walkthrough packet so owner-run
  walkthrough language matches the product surface.
- Left `/proof-lab`, legacy `ProofWorkflowSurface`, endpoint names, artifact
  identifiers such as `report_proof_csv_codebook`, and historical test/helper
  names unchanged.

**Decisions:** UX27 is a frontend/product-copy cleanup only. It does not change
backend contracts, report math, export artifact semantics, widget manifest
semantics, Designer/template authoring, deployment/runtime automation,
real-person data approval, legal/GDPR claims, or platform-canonical
named-instrument content.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-053 remains a real-person/legal-use gate
only; it does not block production-grade engineering with synthetic, seed,
demo, or owner-controlled test data.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - UX27 merge, constrained goal, and D104 assessment

**Goal:** Merge UX27 into `main`, create the constrained readiness-cycle goal,
and run the first post-UX27 assessment without unnecessary worktree/service
churn.

**Did:**

- Created the active goal: "Complete up to 10 constrained private-beta readiness
  cycles after UX27, alternating whole-app assessment and one scoped
  implementation slice, while preventing repeated low-value refinement of the
  same area."
- Fast-forward merged `ux27-product-vocabulary` into `main` at `9821d8e`.
- Removed the temporary UX27 worktree and deleted the feature branch.
- Added
  `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/assessments/D104-POST-UX27-PRIVATE-BETA-PRODUCT-READINESS-ASSESSMENT-2026-05-16.md`.
- Added
  `docs/plans/2026-05-16-des01-setup-template-authoring-foundation.md`.
- Updated roadmap/handoff docs so `DES01` is the active queue head.

**Decision:** Select `DES01`, selected-series setup template authoring
foundation. After UX27, another copy/UI refinement is lower leverage. The
normal Setup route can create tenant-private templates, but the template task is
still effectively a fixed three-question seed editor and the default scoring
JSON can drift from edited question codes. DES01 should add editable question
rows, add/remove/reorder controls, row validation, and generated default scoring
over the existing setup APIs.

**Deferred:** Full SurveyJS Creator/final Designer, branching editor, scale
designer, scoring visual builder, report/dashboard/PDF depth, validation-tenant
content depth, deployment/runtime automation, real-person data approval,
legal/GDPR claims, and platform-canonical named-instrument content.

- Post-merge: `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed.
- D104 is documentation/plan only; no product code changed.
- D104 docs: `git diff --check` passed with LF/CRLF warnings only.

**Next:** Implement `DES01` directly on `main` unless isolation becomes useful.
Avoid rebuilding Docker or restarting services unless verification requires a
fresh runtime.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - DES01 setup template authoring foundation

**Goal:** Make selected-series Setup capable of basic tenant-private template
authoring instead of only editing a fixed three-question seed.

**Did:**

- Added `apps/web/src/lib/product/template-authoring.ts` with pure helpers for
  default question rows, add/remove/reorder behavior, row validation,
  create-template request mapping, and mean-scoring JSON generation from current
  Likert rows.
- Added `apps/web/src/lib/product/template-authoring.test.ts`.
- Updated `SelectedSeriesSetupWorkflow.svelte` so the template task has editable
  question rows with add/remove/reorder controls, code/text/type/required/
  reverse-coded editing, and blocking validation errors.
- Kept default scoring JSON synchronized with the current template rows until a
  user manually edits the scoring document; added a regenerate button for manual
  JSON recovery.
- Added a product-surface Playwright contract that edits rows, removes a seeded
  row, asserts request ordinals and codes, checks generated scoring defaults,
  and asserts the submitted scoring document uses the current row list.

**Decisions:** DES01 stays frontend-first over existing setup APIs. It does not
add backend contracts, full SurveyJS Creator/final Designer, branching editor,
section editor, scale designer, scoring visual builder, template publishing,
canonical named-instrument content, report/dashboard/PDF depth, deployment
automation, real-person data approval, or legal/GDPR claims.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - SEEDUX01 starter content taxonomy and sample labels

**Goal:** Add durable sample-vs-own study metadata and first visible labels so
later self-serve UX slices can safely organize starter content.

**Did:**

- Added `CampaignSeriesStudyKinds`, `CampaignSeriesSampleScenarios`,
  `StudyKind`, `SampleScenario`, `IsSample`, domain validation, EF mapping, and
  migration `20260516093653_AddCampaignSeriesSampleMetadata`.
- Added database check constraints for known study kinds, known sample
  scenarios, and sample/own scenario consistency.
- Projected `studyKind`, `isSample`, `sampleScenario`, and `readOnlyReason`
  through workspace overview recent series, campaign-series list/hub, and
  selected-series setup/operations/reports/waves contracts/read stores.
- Made series-level rename/archive/restore return
  `campaign_series.sample_read_only` for sample studies.
- Extended validation demo catalog and seed script so each tenant marks the
  main starter series as `mixed_lifecycle` and linked-wave series as
  `longitudinal` sample studies after normal API creation.
- Added frontend API types, view-model ownership labels, campaign-series
  portfolio badges/read-only messages, selected-series sample banners, and
  sample-control hiding for rename/archive/restore.
- Added browser coverage proving a sample study and an own study are labelled,
  sample rows hide portfolio mutations, and selected-series hub/child routes
  show the sample read-only state.
- Updated `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, backlog, current roadmap,
  frontend product-surface docs, and self-serve UX contract so `D116` is the
  current queue head.

**Decisions:** SEEDUX01 is a foundation slice. It deliberately does not add
duplicate-to-edit, full starter fixture refresh, home/navigation/list
regrouping, setup/launch/report mutation guard expansion beyond current
series-level portfolio actions, real-person data approval, legal/GDPR claims,
deployment automation, or platform-canonical named-instrument content.

only and does not block synthetic/demo/sample/owner-controlled product work.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - HOME02 self-serve home cockpit

**Goal:** Turn `/app` into a self-serve cockpit that separates sample studies
from own studies and teaches the prepare/collect/review/export lifecycle before
technical workspace detail.

- Extended `WorkspaceOverviewResponse` with `studyCollections.sampleStudies`
  and `studyCollections.ownStudies`.
- Derived active sample and own home collections from existing
  campaign-series read rows while preserving `RecentSeries` and the backend
  command center.
- Added frontend API/view-model support for lifecycle steps, sample study
  cards, own study cards, and state-based next links.
- Changed `/app` from a workspace/read-model page into `Study cockpit`:
  lifecycle phases first, Sample studies before Your studies, workspace totals
  below study sections, and suggested next actions below totals.
- Removed raw permission-code rows from home command cards.
- Updated Playwright coverage for the new first-session home contract and
  updated existing home/product-vocabulary route expectations.
- Updated `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, backlog, current roadmap,
  frontend product-surface docs, and self-serve UX docs so `D117` is the
  current queue head.

**Decisions:** HOME02 preserves existing read models and routes rather than
adding a separate demo route. Sample studies remain normal tenant data and are
shown as read-only examples. HOME02 deliberately does not add duplicate-to-edit,
global navigation redesign, campaign-series portfolio grouping, selected-series
hub rewrite, new starter fixtures, deeper sample mutation guards beyond
existing series-level controls, real-person data approval, production
legal/GDPR claims, deployment automation, or platform-canonical named-instrument
content.

only and does not block synthetic/demo/sample/owner-controlled product work.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - NAV02 simplified global navigation

**Goal:** Make authenticated product navigation understandable by grouping
global routes by user intent and separating selected-study routes from global
modules.

**Did:**

- Refactored `SurfaceNav` from one flat list into `Studies`, `People and
  access`, `Workspace admin`, conditional `Selected study`, and flag-gated
  `Internal tools` groups.
- Renamed visible global nav labels to `Home`, `Studies`, `Instrument library`,
  `Exports`, `Directory`, `Team`, and `Settings` while preserving route URLs.
- Renamed selected-study shell links to `Overview`, `Setup`, `Collect`,
  `Results`, and `Waves` and kept the in-page selected-series workspace nav
  unchanged.
- Kept `/app/demo` hidden from default navigation and rendered `Demo fixtures`
  under `Internal tools` only when `PUBLIC_DEMO_SURFACES_ENABLED=true`.
- Updated browser route contracts plus handoff docs, backlog, current roadmap,
  self-serve UX docs, and frontend product-surface docs so `D118` is the next
  queue head.

**V1 reference review:** Checked v1 `AdminPortal/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`
and `UserPortal/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`. Useful lesson: v1 already used
role/context separation and grouped admin options instead of treating every
destination as the same kind of top-level route. NAV02 ports that lesson as
intent groups, not v1 markup or code.

**Decisions:** Keep backend contracts and routes stable. This slice changes
wayfinding only; it does not change selected-series route content,
campaign-series list grouping, selected-series hub lifecycle shape,
duplicate-to-edit, sample mutation guard depth beyond existing controls,
real-person data approval, production legal/GDPR claims, deployment automation,
or platform-canonical named-instrument content.

**Open questions raised:** None. Q-053 remains a real-person/legal-use gate
only and does not block synthetic/demo/sample/owner-controlled product work.

## 2026-05-16 - codex - SERIES03 campaign-series list lifecycle and ownership grouping

**Goal:** Make `/app/campaign-series` understandable as a study portfolio by
separating sample studies from own studies and surfacing lifecycle state plus
next action before dense filters.

**Did:**

- Added view-model red coverage for `toCampaignSeriesListView` grouping by
  Sample studies, Your studies, lifecycle, and primary action.
- Added browser red coverage for `/app/campaign-series` rendering as `Studies`
  / `Study portfolio`, hiding old technical framing, showing lifecycle labels,
  preserving sample read-only controls, and preserving NAV02 global navigation.
- Extended `toCampaignSeriesListView` with `studySections`, lifecycle groups,
  and primary action links over existing `GET /campaign-series` fields.
- Reworked `/app/campaign-series` to render grouped Sample studies and Your
  studies before secondary search/readiness/sort/visibility filters.
- Changed create/read-only copy to study language (`Create your study`,
  `Create study`, `Study name`) while keeping the existing setup API and route
  target.
- Preserved sample read-only portfolio behavior and own-study rename/archive/
  restore controls.
- Updated `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, backlog, current roadmap,
  self-serve UX docs, and frontend product-surface docs so `D119` is the next
  queue head.

`AdminPortal/ViewModels/CompanyEvaluationsViewModel.cs`,
`AdminPortal/Controllers/CompaniesController.cs`, and
`UserPortal/wwwroot/js/Individual-stats.js`. Useful lessons: v1 evaluation
lists exposed status, progress, and action affordances instead of only raw
entities, and the user statistics flow split completed vs ongoing evaluations
before merging them for selection. SERIES03 ports that lesson as lifecycle and
ownership grouping over the v2 read model, not v1 markup or code.

**Decisions:** Keep SERIES03 frontend/view-model-only. No backend fields,
migrations, route rewrites, API contract changes, duplicate-to-edit, selected
hub redesign, seed changes, legal/GDPR claims, deployment automation, or
platform-canonical named-instrument content were added.

only and does not block synthetic/demo/sample/owner-controlled product work.
