## 2026-05-18 - D333 live VPS staging auth/bootstrap hardening

Assessment/task pair: D333 selected live VPS staging deployment-operability because the product repo, VPS clone, DNS, TLS, and Auth0 config were now in play, and the limiting risk had shifted from local release evidence to whether the real staging environment could authenticate repeatably and survive container recreation.

Implemented D333:
- Installed VPS staging `.env` from `deploy/staging/vps.env.example` with real Auth0 values, generated database passwords, and `validatedscale-staging.croat.dev` / `validatedscale-api-staging.croat.dev` origins.
- Brought the VPS Docker Compose stack up behind Nginx/TLS.
- Fixed Linux EF pending-model false positives by making check-constraint strings line-ending stable.
- Fixed OIDC callback generation behind Nginx by honoring forwarded HTTPS headers in VPS mode.
- Fixed OIDC failure fallback so failed Auth0/login resolution redirects to the web origin instead of `/app` on the API origin.
- Seeded the owner Auth0 email as a tenant-scoped staging admin with `setup.manage` and `team.manage` permissions.
- Added `deploy/staging/seed-staging-admin.ps1` as a repeatable, email-parameterized, idempotent staging admin bootstrap script.
- Added a named Docker volume for API data-protection keys at `/root/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys` so auth key material survives API container recreation.

Verification:
- Focused auth integration tests passed 49/49 after forwarded-header and failure-fallback fixes.
- RED focused staging hardening tests failed 2/2; GREEN focused tests passed 2/2.
- Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 86/86.
- Local and VPS Compose config rendering passed.
- `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
- `seed-staging-admin.ps1 -Email <owner-auth0-email>` reran idempotently with seeded counts `1/1/2/1`.
- VPS public smoke returned API health 200, web root 200, unauthenticated session 401, and login 302.
- Docker inspect confirmed API mounts `platform_staging_data_protection_keys` at `/root/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys`.

Remaining risk: This is owner-controlled staging proof only. Q-053 still blocks real-person production legal/GDPR/DPA claims. Q-054 still blocks outbound operational-notification email routing and related claims. Remote authenticated product-spine smoke and target backup/restore proof remain the next likely deployment-operability gaps.

Next: start D334 post-live-VPS staging assessment.

### D334 - Remote staging smoke harness
Assessment: Staging was reachable after D333, but the proof was manual and not repeatable. The remaining deployment/operability gap was a single smoke command that verifies the public spine without relying on a developer remembering each URL and header.
Task: Added `deploy/staging/smoke-remote-staging.ps1` and a packaging contract test. The script verifies API health, web root, unauthenticated session failure, auth-session CORS preflight, Auth0 login redirect status, and HTTPS callback redirect_uri. It also supports an optional `-SessionCookie` branch for authenticated `/auth/session` proof without storing cookies or owner email in repo.
Verification: `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Remote_staging_smoke_checks_public_auth_cors_and_optional_authenticated_session"` passed 1/1. `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File deploy/staging/smoke-remote-staging.ps1 -ApiOrigin 'https://validatedscale-api-staging.croat.dev' -WebOrigin 'https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev'` passed with apiHealth=200, webRoot=200, unauthenticatedSession=401, authSessionCorsPreflight=204, authLoginRedirect=302, and authenticated proof skipped because no SessionCookie was supplied. `dotnet test tests/Platform.UnitTests/Platform.UnitTests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests"` passed 87/87. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: Full authenticated remote smoke still needs a human-supplied browser session cookie or a dedicated non-secret test-auth path; do not store cookies in repo or logs.

### D335 - Export library PDF action readiness
Assessment: Reports/PDF/export infrastructure is already broad, but the tenant-level export library under-advertised existing PDF artifact capabilities. Succeeded campaign-series report PDFs had safe download mechanics elsewhere, and failed PDF artifacts had a retry endpoint, but the inventory surface did not expose retryability and did not advertise PDF downloadability from the library row.
Task: Added export-library contract fields for `retryableCount` and per-artifact `canRetry`, made failed campaign-series report PDF retry advertising setup-manager gated, and allowed succeeded campaign-series report PDFs to be advertised as downloadable when storage/checksum requirements are satisfied. Added Docker-backed read-store coverage proving tenant isolation, manager vs viewer action gating, and no export content/codebook/storage-key leakage.
Verification: RED focused integration test failed at compile on missing `RetryableCount`/`CanRetry`. GREEN focused Docker-backed test `Export_artifact_library_advertises_pdf_download_and_failed_pdf_retry_safely` passed 1/1. Broader `FullyQualifiedName~Export_artifact_library` integration filter passed 2/2. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: This is API/product-surface readiness only. It does not add UI controls, remote authenticated proof, object-store production credentials, SMTP notifications, or Q-053 legal/GDPR readiness.

### D336 - Withdrawal admin action-readiness flags
Assessment: Withdrawal request intake, review, decision, execution, anonymous-token intake, and operational notifications already exist. The next admin-trust gap was that review rows exposed status and counts but required the product shell to infer next allowed actions from status strings. That is brittle around requested/planned/processing/terminal states.
Task: Added status-derived `canApprove`, `canDeny`, and `canExecute` fields to withdrawal request review responses. Requested requests advertise approve/deny only; planned and processing requests advertise execute/resume; denied/completed/failed requests advertise no admin mutation action. Added Docker-backed store coverage and endpoint contract assertions.
Verification: Initial parallel verification hit a build artifact file-lock between simultaneous `dotnet test` commands; rerunning sequentially isolated the real test result. Focused Docker-backed `Withdrawal_request_review_advertises_status_safe_admin_actions` passed 1/1 after fixing the test setup to avoid duplicate tenant seeding. Broader Docker-backed `FullyQualifiedName~Withdrawal_request_review` passed 4/4. `FullyQualifiedName~WithdrawalEndpoint` passed 20/20. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: This is backend/API action readiness only. It does not add withdrawal UI controls, outbound email, legal/GDPR approval, or remote authenticated product-spine proof.

### D337 - Authenticated remote smoke safe cookie ergonomics
Assessment: D334 made public remote staging smoke repeatable, but authenticated proof still required pasting a browser cookie directly into the command and silently skipped when absent. The next VPS hardening gap was making authenticated smoke operator-safe and fail-closed when explicitly required, without storing or printing cookies.
Task: Extended `deploy/staging/smoke-remote-staging.ps1` with `-SessionCookiePath`, `-RequireAuthenticatedSession`, and `STAGING_SESSION_COOKIE` fallback. The script now rejects simultaneous direct cookie and cookie-file input, reads cookie files with `Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath`, fails before any remote call when required auth proof has no cookie, and only prints that a cookie source was resolved, never the cookie value.
Verification: RED focused static test failed on missing `SessionCookiePath`. GREEN focused static test passed 1/1. Required-auth no-cookie runtime check exited 1 with the expected fail-closed message before remote calls. Public remote smoke against `validatedscale-api-staging.croat.dev` / `validatedscale-staging.croat.dev` still passed with apiHealth=200, webRoot=200, unauthenticatedSession=401, authSessionCorsPreflight=204, authLoginRedirect=302, and authenticated proof skipped. Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 88/88. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: Actual authenticated remote proof still requires the owner to supply a browser session cookie via parameter, ignored file, or `STAGING_SESSION_COOKIE`. This does not add a machine-auth bypass, store cookies in repo, or make Q-053/Q-054 claims.

### D338 - VPS target backup/restore rehearsal
Assessment: D337 closed the safe-cookie ergonomics gap, but the next deployment blocker was still target-environment data-safety proof. Local/default backup/restore evidence already existed, but the real VPS lane needed a Linux-native rehearsal that proves the live staging Postgres container can produce a backup and restore it without overwriting the application database.
Task: Added `deploy/staging/backup-restore-vps-smoke.sh`. The script uses the VPS compose stack, creates a temporary custom-format `pg_dump`, creates a temporary restore database in the same Postgres container, restores the dump into that temporary database, verifies `public.tenant` and `public.audit_event` plus public table/relation counts, writes optional safe JSON evidence, and drops the temporary database plus dump on exit. It does not print env values, database credentials, connection strings, host paths, or cookie/session material.
Verification: RED focused static test failed on missing `backup-restore-vps-smoke.sh`. GREEN focused static test passed 1/1. Runtime proof on the VPS passed with backup bytes `333276`, backup SHA-256 `4077e2939d452b9739a0c6e84263ae576e988ea5182210dde2b03d67abfe5699`, restore public table count `53`, restore public relation count `52`, required platform tables present true, and safe evidence written to `/tmp/d338-vps-backup-restore.json`. Public remote smoke after the restore rehearsal passed with apiHealth=200, webRoot=200, unauthenticatedSession=401, authSessionCorsPreflight=204, authLoginRedirect=302, and authenticated proof skipped. Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 89/89. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: This is owner-controlled VPS staging engineering proof only. It is not a production legal/GDPR/DPA claim under Q-053, not outbound operational-notification email readiness under Q-054, and not yet wired into a committed VPS release-evidence command or rollback/redeploy proof.

### D339 - VPS release evidence command
Assessment: D338 proved backup/restore on the target VPS, and D334/D337 proved public remote smoke plus safe authenticated-cookie ergonomics, but operators still needed separate commands and separate evidence paths. The next VPS hardening gap was one owner-runnable Linux command that captures target release evidence without claiming legal/GDPR readiness or operational email readiness.
Task: Added `deploy/staging/run-vps-release-checks.sh`. The runner checks API health, web root, unauthenticated session denial, CORS preflight, and Auth0 login redirect with the same relative `/app` return URL and tenant id contract as the PowerShell remote smoke. It supports optional authenticated proof through `--session-cookie-file` or `STAGING_SESSION_COOKIE`, with `--require-authenticated-session` for fail-closed owner runs. It then runs `backup-restore-vps-smoke.sh` and writes safe `remote-public-smoke.json`, `backup-restore.json`, and `release-evidence.json`.
Verification: RED focused static test failed on missing `run-vps-release-checks.sh`. First VPS runtime attempt exposed an auth-login bug: the bash runner used an absolute web `returnUrl`, while the working PowerShell smoke uses encoded relative `/app` plus `tenantId`. Added a focused regression assertion for `tenant_id`, `returnUrl=%2Fapp`, `tenantId=$tenant_id`, and `X-Tenant-Id`. GREEN focused static test passed 1/1. VPS runtime proof passed from a temporary copy with backup bytes `333276`, backup SHA-256 `182868a0b89233293d1431bcd106f487906b6a2823b789d277bf231f2ab5a841`, restore public table count `53`, restore public relation count `52`, release evidence written under `/tmp/d339-vps-release-evidence`, `remotePublicSmokeProven=true`, `vpsBackupRestoreProven=true`, `authenticatedRemoteSmokeProven=false`, `legalGdprReady=false`, and `operationalNotificationEmailReady=false`. Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 90/90. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: Authenticated remote proof is still not complete until the owner supplies a current browser session cookie. Rollback/redeploy proof and broader safe ops evidence capture remain open. This does not change Q-053 or Q-054.

### D340 - VPS redeploy proof command
Assessment: D339 made release evidence owner-runnable, but the stack still lacked proof that a redeploy/recreate operation can run from the VPS checkout and leave staging healthy. Actual rollback to a previous revision remains a separate risk because there is no selected prior deploy target in the command yet.
Task: Added `deploy/staging/redeploy-vps-stack.sh`. The script records current git revision and branch, runs `docker compose --env-file deploy/staging/.env -f deploy/staging/docker-compose.yml -f deploy/staging/docker-compose.vps.yml up -d --build`, asserts the checkout revision did not change during redeploy, runs `run-vps-release-checks.sh`, and writes safe `redeploy-evidence.json` plus nested release evidence.
Verification: RED focused static test failed on missing `redeploy-vps-stack.sh`. GREEN focused static test passed 1/1. VPS runtime proof from a temporary copy rebuilt/published API, worker, web, and migrator images, recreated/reran migrator/runtime-role/seed services, kept Postgres running/healthy, ran release checks, and wrote evidence under `/tmp/d340-vps-redeploy-evidence`. Evidence recorded before/after revision `2500fdfbed03bd561944e2d5f8e101bb58421e5c`, `redeployProven=true`, `releaseChecksProven=true`, `rollbackProven=false`, `legalGdprReady=false`, and `operationalNotificationEmailReady=false`. Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 91/91. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: This proves redeploy/recreate for the current checkout, not rollback to an earlier revision. Authenticated remote proof still needs an owner-supplied browser session cookie. Q-053 and Q-054 remain unchanged.

### D341 - VPS rollback round-trip proof
Assessment: D340 proved redeploy/recreate but still recorded `rollbackProven=false`. The next VPS hardening gap was an actual rollback round-trip that deploys a previous known-good revision, proves release checks there, restores the current branch/revision, and proves release checks again.
Task: Added `deploy/staging/rollback-vps-stack.sh`. The script requires a clean worktree, defaults rollback target to `HEAD~1`, records/normalizes rollback and restore revisions, checks out the rollback revision detached, rebuilds/recreates the VPS stack, runs `run-vps-release-checks.sh`, checks out the restore branch/revision without `git reset`, rebuilds/recreates the VPS stack again, runs release checks again, and writes safe `rollback-evidence.json`.
Verification: RED focused static test failed on missing `rollback-vps-stack.sh`. GREEN focused static test passed 1/1. VPS runtime proof from a temporary copy rolled back from `742909343f8f0f9fddc1a98715948de4d69ae9d0` to `2500fdfbed03bd561944e2d5f8e101bb58421e5c`, ran release checks with backup/restore and public remote smoke, restored branch `staging` to `742909343f8f0f9fddc1a98715948de4d69ae9d0`, ran release checks again, and left the VPS checkout on `staging...origin/staging`. Evidence recorded `rollbackProven=true`, `restoreProven=true`, `releaseChecksAfterRollbackProven=true`, `releaseChecksAfterRestoreProven=true`, `legalGdprReady=false`, and `operationalNotificationEmailReady=false`. Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 92/92. `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Remaining risk: Authenticated remote proof still needs an owner-supplied browser session cookie. Q-053 and Q-054 remain unchanged. Final completion should audit the goal and not mark complete unless authenticated proof is supplied or explicitly descoped by the owner.

### D342 - VPS hardening completion audit and authenticated remote proof

Assessment: D338-D341 had already added the VPS backup/restore rehearsal, owner-runnable VPS release evidence command, redeploy proof, and rollback/restore round-trip proof. The only remaining hard blocker in the active VPS hardening goal was authenticated remote smoke, which required an owner-controlled browser session cookie that should not be pasted into chat or stored in the repo.

Task: The owner placed the browser cookie on the VPS at `/tmp/staging-session.cookie` with restrictive permissions. Ran the fail-closed authenticated VPS release command from `/opt/instruments-platform`:

`bash deploy/staging/run-vps-release-checks.sh --evidence-dir /tmp/d342-authenticated-vps-release-evidence --session-cookie-file /tmp/staging-session.cookie --require-authenticated-session`

- VPS checkout was `staging...origin/staging` at `d220e5172ff5a0e59648f456b5579d5b58fc2c60`.
- Cookie file existed as `/tmp/staging-session.cookie`, mode `rw-------`, size `1398`.
- The authenticated VPS release command exited 0 and printed `Authenticated remote smoke: passed`.
- `/tmp/d342-authenticated-vps-release-evidence/release-evidence.json` recorded status `passed`, gates `remote-public-smoke`, `vps-backup-restore-smoke`, and `authenticated-remote-smoke` all passed, with `remotePublicSmokeProven=true`, `vpsBackupRestoreProven=true`, and `authenticatedRemoteSmokeProven=true`.
- `/tmp/d342-authenticated-vps-release-evidence/remote-public-smoke.json` recorded API health 200, web root 200, unauthenticated session 401, auth-session CORS preflight 204, Auth0 login redirect 302, and authenticated session proof passed/proven true.
- `/tmp/d342-authenticated-vps-release-evidence/backup-restore.json` recorded backup bytes `333277`, backup SHA-256 `1e90fecec10a03b2a8e03f84ca37854f23c61716109b9c48bc75e64a5753e357`, restore public table count `53`, restore public relation count `52`, and required platform tables present true.
- Prior committed VPS evidence remained present: `/tmp/d340-vps-redeploy-evidence-committed/redeploy-evidence.json` recorded `redeployProven=true` and `releaseChecksProven=true`; `/tmp/d341-vps-rollback-evidence-committed/rollback-evidence.json` recorded `rollbackProven=true`, `restoreProven=true`, `releaseChecksAfterRollbackProven=true`, and `releaseChecksAfterRestoreProven=true`.

Completion audit result: The active VPS hardening objective is complete for owner-controlled staging rehearsal evidence. It proves authenticated remote smoke, target backup/restore, owner-runnable VPS release evidence, redeploy, rollback/restore, and safe evidence capture. The evidence deliberately preserves `legalGdprReady=false` and `operationalNotificationEmailReady=false`; Q-053 still blocks real-person production legal/GDPR/DPA claims, and Q-054 still blocks outbound operational-notification email workflows/claims. No standalone UX work was added.

Next: start D343 post-VPS-hardening product/runtime assessment.

### D343 - Post-VPS-hardening product/runtime assessment

Assessment: D342 completed the active VPS hardening thread. The VPS rehearsal lane now has authenticated `/auth/session` proof, target backup/restore proof, an owner-runnable release evidence command, redeploy proof, rollback/restore proof, and safe evidence boundaries. The remaining useful question is no longer "can the VPS be operated safely?" but "what product/runtime proof is weakest now that the VPS is trustworthy enough to rehearse on?"

Findings:
- The active roadmap remains the generic tenant-private proof spine, not platform-canonical named-instrument content.
- Q-053 still blocks real-person production legal/GDPR/DPA claims, but does not block production-grade engineering with synthetic, seed, demo, or owner-controlled test data.
- Q-054 still blocks outbound operational-notification email routing, requester/admin email workflows, notification preference UI, SMTP-backed operational alerting, and any claim that operational events are emailed.
- `deploy/staging/run-vps-release-checks.sh` proves remote public smoke, authenticated `/auth/session`, and target backup/restore on the VPS.
- `deploy/staging/smoke-product-spine.ps1` proves the much broader QA01 product spine, but its authenticated branch is still local/dev-auth oriented and the evidence limitation still treats remote VPS proof as separate.
- More VPS redeploy/rollback hardening would be diminishing returns compared with proving the full product spine against the real VPS target under real Auth0 session cookies.

Decision: Selected D344 authenticated VPS product-spine smoke harness. This is an agent-executable runtime proof slice, not a UX slice and not a legal/compliance claim.

Acceptance target for D344:
- Keep the current local/dev product-spine smoke path working.
- Add an owner-runnable authenticated remote path for the real VPS API/web origins.
- Read browser cookies only from an ignored file or `STAGING_SESSION_COOKIE`; do not print, persist in repo, or include them in evidence.
- Fail closed before remote calls when authenticated remote proof is explicitly required but no cookie is supplied.
- Use synthetic/demo/owner-controlled data only.
- Write safe evidence that omits cookies, raw headers, response bodies, participant codes, withdrawal tokens, answers, storage keys, credential values, connection strings, tenant ids, and email addresses.
- Keep `legalGdprReady=false` and `operationalNotificationEmailReady=false` or equivalent claim-boundary wording.

Verification: Docs-only assessment. Added assessment `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/assessments/D343-POST-VPS-HARDENING-PRODUCT-RUNTIME-ASSESSMENT-2026-05-18.md`, plan `docs/plans/2026-05-18-d344-authenticated-vps-product-spine-smoke.md`, moved the queue head to D344, and updated this handoff log. No product code or runtime was changed.

Remaining risk: D344 may expose API assumptions in the product-spine smoke that were hidden by local/dev auth. If the owner browser cookie expires before runtime proof, the implementation can still complete safely but final VPS proof remains owner-cookie dependent. Q-053 and Q-054 remain unchanged.

Next: implement D344 test-first.

### D344 - Authenticated VPS product-spine smoke harness

Assessment/task pair: D343 selected D344 because D342 made the VPS trustworthy enough to rehearse on, but the full QA01 product-spine proof still used local/dev auth rather than a real Auth0 browser session against the VPS target.

Implemented D344:
- Extended `deploy/staging/smoke-product-spine.ps1` with `-SessionCookie`, `-SessionCookiePath`, `-RequireAuthenticatedSession`, and `STAGING_SESSION_COOKIE` support.
- Added `Resolve-SessionCookie` with conflict rejection, ignored-file guidance, environment fallback, and fail-closed required-auth behavior.
- Added remote-cookie auth mode that fetches `/auth/csrf`, merges any returned CSRF cookie, sends `Cookie` plus `X-CSRF-TOKEN` for tenant-member mutations, and uses a narrow `HttpClient` path when a raw browser cookie must be sent from Windows PowerShell.
- Preserved the existing local/dev auth path when no session cookie is supplied.
- Added safe `authProof` fields to product-spine evidence: mode, authenticated session proof, setup.manage proof, CSRF token proof, and cookie-source proof.
- Updated the VPS staging runbook with the authenticated VPS product-spine command and Q-053/Q-054 boundaries.

Verification:
- RED focused static test `Product_spine_smoke_supports_authenticated_remote_cookie_sources` failed on missing `SessionCookiePath`.
- GREEN focused static test passed 1/1.
- Required-auth no-cookie command exited 1 before remote calls with `Authenticated product-spine session proof required...`.
- The first remote attempt exposed that Windows PowerShell was not sending the raw Cookie header reliably through `Invoke-WebRequest`/`Invoke-RestMethod`; the implementation switched cookie-authenticated API calls to `HttpClient`.
- Authenticated VPS product-spine smoke passed against `https://validatedscale-api-staging.croat.dev` and `https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev`, using the owner cookie loaded from `/tmp/staging-session.cookie` in memory only. Evidence was written to `%TEMP%\d344-authenticated-vps-product-spine.json`.
- Safe evidence extraction recorded status `passed`, auth mode `remoteCookieAuthenticated`, authenticated session true, setup.manage permission true, CSRF token true, cookie source true, campaign-series/report/export/PDF/withdrawal/operational-notification/campaign-invitation milestones true, `inAppOnly=true`, `emailRoutingProven=false`, `smtpDeliveryProven=false`, and `failedRequeueRecoveryProven=false`.
- Full `StagingWorkerDeploymentPackageTests` passed 93/93 after preserving the historical `Invoke-AuthenticatedDevSession` hook name as a compatibility wrapper.
- `dotnet build --no-restore` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Remaining risk: Q-053 still blocks real-person production legal/GDPR/DPA claims. Q-054 still blocks outbound operational-notification email workflows and claims. The browser cookie remains owner-controlled and may expire between runs.

Next: start D345 assessment.

### D345 - Post-D344 product/runtime assessment

Assessment: D344 moved the broad QA01 product-spine proof from local/dev auth to the real VPS rehearsal lane with owner-cookie auth and CSRF. The VPS target now has infrastructure proof, authenticated session proof, target backup/restore proof, redeploy/rollback proof, and broad product-spine proof. Another smoke-only hardening slice would have diminishing return unless a live failure appears.

Findings:
- Owner-only O01/O02/O03 remain the highest business priority, but an agent cannot run those conversations.
- Q-053 still blocks real-person production legal/GDPR/DPA claims, but does not block synthetic/demo engineering proof.
- Q-054 still blocks outbound operational-notification email workflows and claims.
- The current proof demo brief still says the proof is local/dev and says not to claim production auth/deployment, PDFs, withdrawal, or several paths that are now engineering-proven in the owner-controlled VPS rehearsal lane.
- The owner blocker action pack and validation walkthrough packet still frame the current proof mostly around local setup and validation tenant preflight. They do not yet make D342/D344 evidence the default validation-call posture.

Decision: Selected D346 VPS-current validation packet refresh. This is a docs-only owner-validation readiness slice. It should update the owner-facing validation materials to accurately say what can be shown now: live VPS staging, Auth0-backed owner session, CSRF-protected tenant-member product-spine paths, report/export/PDF, withdrawal lifecycle, in-app operational notifications, campaign invitation local-dev delivery proof, target backup/restore, redeploy, and rollback/restore. It must keep the claim boundaries explicit: proof/demo data only, no real-person legal/GDPR/DPA readiness, no outbound operational-notification email readiness, no SLA/managed-hosting claim, no platform-canonical named-instrument claim, and no official score/clinical/OSH advice.

Verification: Docs-only assessment. Added assessment `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/assessments/D345-POST-D344-PRODUCT-RUNTIME-ASSESSMENT-2026-05-18.md`, plan `docs/plans/2026-05-18-d346-vps-current-validation-packet-refresh.md`, moved the queue head to D346, and updated handoff notes. No product code or runtime changed.

Remaining risk: Owner still must run O01/O02/O03. D346 only prevents stale validation material from underclaiming or misclaiming the current proof.

Next: implement D346 docs-only.

Next: start D347 assessment.

### D347 - Post-D346 product/runtime assessment

Assessment: D346 completed the owner-facing validation packet refresh. The project now has a current live VPS rehearsal lane, authenticated product-spine proof, backup/restore proof, redeploy/rollback proof, and owner-call material that accurately reflects those proofs. O01/O02/O03 remain owner-only and are now the highest-leverage next step.

Decision: pause default agent execution. No unblocked agent-executable product/runtime slice is selected by default.

Reasoning:
- More VPS hardening is low leverage after D342/D344 unless a live failure appears.
- More validation docs are low leverage after D346 unless owner feedback exposes a missing call asset.
- More product/report/setup/runtime work before O01/O02/O03 feedback risks building past the market signal.
- Q-053 blocks real-person production legal/GDPR/DPA claims, not synthetic/demo engineering; Q-054 blocks outbound operational-notification email work; neither creates a better default engineering slice right now.
- The v1 failure mode was distribution/validation, not lack of code. The current project should not hide that risk behind another agent-executable queue item.

Resume conditions:
- Owner provides O01/O02/O03 call notes or objections.
- Owner asks for a specific implementation slice.
- Owner reopens a deferred task such as deployment automation or UI/UX assessment.
- A live VPS/product smoke fails and needs diagnosis.

Verification: Docs-only assessment. Added `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/assessments/D347-POST-D346-PRODUCT-RUNTIME-ASSESSMENT-2026-05-18.md`, updated `NEXT-ACTIONS.md`, `NEXT-SESSION-PLAN.md`, and this log. No product code changed and no tests/build were required.

Remaining risk: O01/O02/O03 are still open. The next meaningful progress depends on owner action or concrete feedback.

Next: owner runs validation outreach/calls, or explicitly selects the next engineering task.

### 2026-05-18 - Registration onboarding tenant-session repair

Assessment: Live local onboarding exposed a registration dead-end after Auth0 signup: the app returned to `/app` but `/auth/session` still required an explicit tenant header, while the newly issued tenant session only carried the tenant membership claim. API logs also showed local Auth0 database users being rejected when `email_verified=false`, and the unauthenticated `/app` CTA had no tenant target.

Task: Moved tenant-context resolution after authentication, allowed tenant context to be inferred only from exactly one authenticated tenant-membership claim when no `X-Tenant-Id` header is present, added regression coverage for middleware and `/auth/session`, and changed the `/app` unauthenticated fallback to send tenantless users back to workspace creation instead of a dead tenant login.

Verification: Focused integration session regression passed 2/2. Focused tenant-context unit tests passed 9/9. Local staging rebuilt successfully with web production build passing. Live local checks returned `/register` 200, `/health/ready` 200, anonymous `/registration/session` 401, and registration Auth0 redirect still included `prompt=login` plus `screen_hint=signup`. The local API container was started with `Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=false` so unverified Auth0 database users can complete local testing.

Remaining risk: Production/staging env examples still default `Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=true`; that is deliberate until owner decides whether verified email is required for real signups.

### 2026-05-18 - Registration workspace creation audit fix

Assessment: Local registration reached the signed-in registration session, but workspace creation returned the generic unavailable message. API logs showed `Tenant context is required to write audit events` while `RegistrationWorkspaceService.EnsurePermissionAsync` dynamically created missing global `Permission` rows before a tenant context existed.

Task: Excluded global `Permission` reference rows from tenant audit event creation. Tenant-scoped registration rows still audit after the workspace tenant context is established.

Verification: Rebuilt local staging successfully. Live local checks returned `/health/ready` 200 and `/register` 200. The local API container remains configured with `Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=false` for unverified Auth0 database-user testing.

Remaining risk: The final workspace POST requires the owner's browser registration cookie, so user retry is the live confirmation for the full Auth0 browser flow.

### 2026-05-19 - Staging registration callback hotfix

Assessment: Owner reported `/register` -> Continue with account -> Auth0 -> `/app?auth=failed`. Fresh VPS API logs showed `OIDC login rejected because no platform tenant membership was resolved`, and the running API env showed `Registration__Enabled=false`. Earlier verified-email rejection was fixed, but the browser was still reaching the existing-tenant login path and staging registration itself was disabled.

Task:
- Set VPS staging env `Registration__Enabled=true`.
- Set `Registration__BetaAccessCodeSha256Hashes__0` for `martin-beta-2026`.
- Kept `Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=false` for beta database-user testing.
- Patched `/register` so the Continue-with-account CTA cannot fall back to `PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGIN_URL` / existing-workspace tenant login while the registration URL is preparing.
- Commit: `55dbda9 fix(registration): prevent tenant login fallback`, pushed to `main` and `staging`; rebuilt/restarted staging web and API.

Verification:
- API `/health/ready` returned 200.
- Web `/register` returned 200.
- Direct `/auth/login?registration=1&returnUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fvalidatedscale-staging.croat.dev%2Fregister` returned 302 to Auth0 with `prompt=login` and `screen_hint=signup`.
- Running API env confirms `Registration__Enabled=true` and `Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=false`.

Remaining risk: owner must retry the browser Auth0 flow. If it still lands on `/app?auth=failed`, inspect fresh API logs for the next OIDC rejection reason before making another change.

### 2026-05-19 - Registration switch-account hotfix

Assessment: Owner confirmed browser/Auth0 session reuse remained even after refresh/private-window attempts. Registration needed an explicit account-switch path and Auth0 account selection instead of relying on refresh or local app-cookie logout.

Task:
- Added backend support for registration bootstrap to honor `prompt=select_account` instead of forcing `prompt=login` for every non-tenant login.
- Added regression coverage: `Login_endpoint_forwards_select_account_prompt_for_registration_bootstrap`.
- Updated `/register` registration login URL to include `prompt=select_account`.
- Added visible `Switch account` action in the signed-in registration state. It clears the app/registration cookie through `/auth/logout` and returns to `/register`.
- Commit: `028f439 fix(registration): support account switching`, pushed to `main` and `staging`, rebuilt/restarted staging API and web.

Verification:
- RED focused test failed before code because `prompt=select_account` was not forwarded.
- PASS: `dotnet test tests\\Platform.IntegrationTests\\Platform.IntegrationTests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~AuthEndpointTests.Login_endpoint_forwards_select_account_prompt_for_registration_bootstrap|FullyQualifiedName~AuthEndpointTests.Login_endpoint_starts_registration_bootstrap_with_signup_hint"` (2 passed).
- VPS web production build passed during Docker build.
- Public checks: API `/health/ready` 200, web `/register` 200.
- Direct registration login challenge now returns Auth0 redirect with both `prompt=select_account` and `screen_hint=signup`.

### 2026-05-19 - Provider logout for registration account switching

Assessment: Owner confirmed the previous Switch account action looped because it only cleared the platform app cookie; Auth0 SSO still reused the same upstream account.

Task:
- Added optional `/auth/logout?provider=1&returnUrl=...` behavior. Normal `/auth/logout` remains local app-cookie logout only.
- Provider logout validates the same safe return URL, revokes/clears the app session, then redirects to the configured OIDC authority `/v2/logout` with `client_id` and encoded `returnTo`.
- Updated `/register` Switch account link to request provider logout.
- Added regression coverage for provider logout redirect while preserving local-only logout behavior.
- Commit: `41dd64b fix(auth): clear provider session for account switch`, pushed to `main` and `staging`, rebuilt/restarted staging API and web.

Verification:
- PASS: focused auth tests for provider logout, local logout, and registration select-account prompt (3 passed).
- VPS web production build passed.
- Public API `/health/ready` returned 200.
- Direct staging `/auth/logout?provider=1&returnUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fvalidatedscale-staging.croat.dev%2Fregister` returns 302 to `https://instrument.eu.auth0.com/v2/logout?...&returnTo=https%3A%2F%2Fvalidatedscale-staging.croat.dev%2Fregister` and expires `__Host-instruments-platform`.
- Running staging commit: `41dd64b`; API image `a98ecea94901`, web image `4ca1c8f8b582`.

Remaining risk: Auth0 tenant must allow `https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev/register` in Allowed Logout URLs. If Auth0 shows an error after Switch account, add that exact URL in Auth0 settings or change the returnTo to an already-allowed URL.

### 2026-05-19 - Registration switch logout return URL hotfix

Assessment: Auth0 rejected provider logout with `returnTo=https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev/register`, causing the Auth0 Oops page. Direct probe confirmed Auth0 accepts `returnTo=https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev/`.

Task:
- Changed `/register` Switch account link to call `/auth/logout?provider=1` with root web return URL instead of `/register`.
- Commit: `35bc0bd fix(registration): use allowed provider logout return`, pushed to `main` and `staging`, rebuilt/restarted staging web.

- Direct staging `/auth/logout?provider=1&returnUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fvalidatedscale-staging.croat.dev%2F` returns 302 to Auth0 `/v2/logout` with root `returnTo` and expires `__Host-instruments-platform`.
- Web `/register` returns 200.
- Running staging commit: `35bc0bd`; web image `0769d9a12e08`.

Behavior note: Switch account now returns to the landing page after Auth0 logout because that is the Auth0-allowed logout URL. Owner should click Create workspace again after provider logout returns.

### 2026-05-19 - Auto-resume registration after provider logout

Assessment: Owner asked why Switch account returns to the landing page instead of directly to sign-in. Direct Auth0 `/u/login?state=...` is not a stable URL; it is generated only after a fresh `/authorize` challenge. Auth0 accepts root logout return URLs with a query marker.

Task:
- Changed Switch account to use provider logout `returnTo=https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev/?postLogout=register`.
- Added root-page client behavior: when `postLogout=register` is present, immediately start `/auth/login?registration=1&returnUrl=<web /register>&prompt=select_account` through the API/auth origin.
- Commit: `e9ea9c7 fix(registration): resume signup after provider logout`, pushed to `main` and `staging`, rebuilt/restarted staging web.

Verification:
- VPS web production build passed.
- Direct staging provider logout URL now redirects to Auth0 `/v2/logout` with `returnTo=https%3A%2F%2Fvalidatedscale-staging.croat.dev%2F%3FpostLogout%3Dregister` and expires `__Host-instruments-platform`.
- Web `/?postLogout=register` returns 200 and client-side code will immediately start the registration login challenge.
- Running staging commit: `e9ea9c7`; web image `70af188abf9f`.

## 2026-05-19 - registration switch account signup prompt
- Assessment: Switch-account cleared provider SSO, then resumed registration through a challenge URL that forced account selection. That solved stale SSO but made Auth0 present sign-in/account-pick behavior instead of the signup-first registration intent.
- Task: Updated registration bootstrap links so switch-account returns to root with postLogout=register, then root resumes /auth/login?registration=1&returnUrl=/register without prompt=select_account. The API registration bootstrap default now controls the challenge with prompt=login and screen_hint=signup.
- Verification: Pending staging deploy/check for commit 36ef94c.
- Risk: Auth0 Universal Login may still visually prefer login depending on tenant configuration even when screen_hint=signup is present.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 36ef94c. / and /register returned 200. /auth/login?registration=1 returned 302 with prompt=login and screen_hint=signup and no select_account. Provider logout returned 302 to Auth0 /v2/logout with returnTo=https://validatedscale-staging.croat.dev/?postLogout=register and cleared __Host-instruments-platform.

## 2026-05-19 - auth failed email verification reminder
- Assessment: /app?auth=failed currently drops the owner into a generic workspace sign-in state. One common cause after signup is unverified provider email, but the UI did not tell the user what to do next.
- Task: Added a visible email-verification reminder on the app auth boundary when the URL contains auth=failed, while preserving the generic workspace sign-in message for wrong-account or missing-membership cases. Added a targeted Playwright expectation for the failed-auth reminder.
- Verification: Not run locally in this pass; staging web Docker build will validate the frontend bundle during deploy.
- Risk: The message is intentionally broad because auth=failed can also mean wrong account, missing membership, declined consent, or provider mismatch.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 6b10949. Web Docker build passed, /app?auth=failed returned 200, /register returned 200, and web image 8a5975264473 was running.

## 2026-05-19 - auth failed sign-out action
- Assessment: The /app?auth=failed email-verification reminder mentioned signing out, but the unauthenticated failed-auth action row only had the primary auth action and Retry. That left the recovery copy ahead of the available UI.
- Task: Added a visible Sign out link to the auth=failed unauthenticated app boundary and updated the Playwright expectation for that failed-auth state.
- Verification: Not run locally; staging web Docker build/deploy will provide bundle validation.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 89e012d. Web Docker build passed, /app?auth=failed returned 200, and web image 8074cd8a4360 was running.

## 2026-05-19 - auth failed marker cleanup
- Assessment: A browser could recover from /app?auth=failed through an existing valid app session, but the stale auth=failed query marker remained in the address bar after the app authenticated. That made a healthy workspace look like it was still in a failed-auth state.
- Task: Updated the app auth boundary to remove auth=failed from the URL with history.replaceState after /auth/session succeeds. Added a targeted Playwright expectation that /app?auth=failed canonicalizes to /app when the session is valid.
- Verification: Not run locally; staging web Docker build/deploy will validate the frontend bundle.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 2730173. Web Docker build passed, /app?auth=failed returned 200, and web image 16557edcb32d was running.

## 2026-05-19 - failed-auth complete logout action
- Assessment: The failed-auth Sign out action used normal local logout, which clears only the platform app session and returns home. That is misleading in the Auth0 stale/wrong-account recovery path because Auth0 SSO can remain active.
- Task: Changed the failed-auth recovery action to Sign out completely and generate /auth/logout?provider=1&returnUrl=<web root>, preserving normal local logout elsewhere. Updated the Playwright expectation to require provider logout on the failed-auth recovery link.
- Verification: Not run locally; staging web Docker build/deploy and direct provider-logout probe will validate the route shape.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit dd8d16a. Web Docker build passed, /app?auth=failed returned 200, direct provider logout returned 302 to Auth0 /v2/logout and expired __Host-instruments-platform, and web image f137fda14e11 was running.

## 2026-05-19 - failed-auth registration recovery path
- Assessment: Owner verified a new Auth0 email after landing on /app?auth=failed, then clicked Retry and stayed stuck. Staging API logs showed OIDC login rejected because no platform tenant membership was resolved, not an active email-verification rejection. The failed-auth Retry button only rechecked /auth/session, so it could not create the missing workspace membership.
- Task: Changed the /app?auth=failed unauthenticated recovery UI so the primary action is Continue workspace setup -> /register. Updated copy to explain that email verification alone does not create workspace membership, kept existing-workspace sign-in as secondary, kept provider Sign out completely, and removed the failed-auth Retry button. Updated the targeted Playwright expectation.
- Verification: Not run locally; staging web Docker build/deploy will validate the frontend bundle.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 3f5527a. Web Docker build passed, /app?auth=failed returned 200, /register returned 200, and web image 0596e1429601 was running.

## 2026-05-19 - registration wording cleanup
- Assessment: The registration page used owner-account language before the user had a workspace, which made the normal signup path feel like a special/admin flow. Owner semantics still matter after workspace creation, but not as the primary CTA label.
- Task: Reworded the signed-out registration state to normal signup language: Create account, Continue registration, and workspace setup. Kept the ready-state ownership meaning softer as workspace management instead of owner-account wording.
- Verification: Not run locally; staging web Docker build/deploy will validate the frontend bundle.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 453ef15. Web Docker build passed, /register returned 200, and web image bae5f4911600 was running.

## 2026-05-19 - verified email enforcement recovery
- Assessment: Staging had Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=false during registration debugging. Re-enabling it without a reasoned redirect would make unverified signup failures look like generic auth failures. The correct production-grade behavior is to reject unverified provider emails, preserve an email_unverified auth reason, and route registration users back to /register with a clear verify-email recovery action.
- Task: Tagged OIDC unverified-email failures with auth=email_unverified, preserved that reason through RemoteFailure redirects, and added /register handling that tells users to verify email then continue registration. Updated auth tests for the failure reason and remote-failure redirect shape.
- Verification: Pending deploy, staging env flip to RequireVerifiedEmail=true, and targeted probes.
- Staging verification: Deployed commit 8a95190. API and web Docker builds passed, API/web containers were recreated, running API env reports Authentication__Oidc__RequireVerifiedEmail=true, /health/ready returned 200, /register?auth=email_unverified returned 200, and registration auth challenge still returns 302 with prompt=login and screen_hint=signup. Focused auth tests passed 3/3 before nullable-warning cleanup.
- Follow-up verification: Focused auth tests passed 3/3 again after nullable-warning cleanup, with no compile warnings from the edited assertions.

## 2026-05-19 - registration verified-email flow repair

Assessment: The registration path was split between a legacy account-first bootstrap flow and the registration-intent flow. When verified email was required, failed OIDC callbacks could return to `/app?auth=failed`, losing the pending workspace setup context and forcing users back through signup.

Task: Switched `/register` to collect email, workspace name, and beta code before Auth0, create a registration intent, store the pending continuation URL in session storage, and show a clear "Verify email, then finish setup" recovery CTA. Updated OIDC remote failure recovery so unverified registration-token/bootstrap callbacks return to `/register?auth=email_unverified`. Added signup/login hints to registration intent login URLs.

Verification: Added red/green regression coverage for unverified registration recovery. Ran targeted AuthEndpointTests (4 passed), Docker-backed RegistrationLoginResolverTests for successful intent consumption and mismatched email rejection (2 passed), and SvelteKit production build via the local Node executable (passed; existing large chunk warning only).

Remaining risk: Browser-level Auth0 email verification depends on Auth0's live session/claim refresh behavior, so staging manual sign-up should still be exercised with a new mailbox after deploy.

## 2026-05-19 - failed-auth verified signup recovery action

Assessment: After the registration-intent flow, `/app?auth=failed` still showed the older `Continue workspace setup` recovery and the secondary existing-workspace sign-in could use the static staging tenant login target. For a newly registered workspace, the only valid recovery after email verification is the pending registration-token login URL stored from `/register`.

Task: Updated the app auth boundary to read `instruments-platform.pending-registration-login-url` from session storage, use it as the failed-auth primary sign-in target, remove the stale `Continue workspace setup` CTA, reword the reminder to `Verify email, then sign in`, and clear the pending URL only after `/auth/session` succeeds. Added Playwright coverage for the pending-registration failed-auth recovery.

Verification: RED focused Playwright expectation failed against the old page. GREEN focused Playwright test passed after the app-boundary fix. Web production build passed via direct Node/Vite invocation; existing large chunk warning only.

Remaining risk: If the browser loses session storage before the user verifies email, the failed-auth page can still only fall back to the configured tenant login/create-workspace route. Provider logout remains the safe recovery for wrong/stale Auth0 SSO.

## 2026-05-19 - duplicate-email beta registration policy

Assessment: Owner accepted that beta registration should not allow the same platform email to create duplicate workspaces. Auth0 should remain the identity provider only; platform data should decide workspace membership and duplicate registration policy.

Task: Closed Q-055 in `OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`: for M1/beta, one normalized email may belong to one active platform workspace membership. Registration intent creation should reject valid attempts for an email already present as any workspace member, return a clear workspace-exists message, and provide a sign-in path with `login_hint` set to the attempted email. Future multi-workspace support requires a workspace picker/account switcher design before this rule is relaxed.

Verification: Docs-only update; no runtime verification run.

## 2026-05-19 - duplicate-email registration validation implementation

Assessment: Q-055 closed the beta policy that one normalized email may belong to one active platform workspace membership. The registration intent endpoint still allowed duplicate workspace attempts for an existing platform email.

Task: Added duplicate-email validation in `POST /registration/intents` after beta-code and return-url validation. The service now checks active platform user accounts, returns `409 registration.email_exists`, includes a safe existing-workspace `loginUrl` with `tenantId`, `returnUrl`, `prompt=login`, and `login_hint`, and does not create a new registration intent. The register page now surfaces the conflict as `A workspace already exists for this email. Sign in instead.` with a primary `Sign in instead` link and a `Use a different email` reset action. Shared `Error` now carries problem-details extensions for safe structured recovery links.

Verification: RED endpoint/service tests failed before implementation. GREEN focused endpoint conflict test passed 1/1. GREEN Docker-backed registration intent duplicate-email test passed 1/1. Combined focused backend tests passed 2/2. Web production build passed via direct Node/Vite invocation; existing large chunk warning only.

Remaining risk: Staging should be probed after deploy with an already-seeded member email to confirm the live API returns 409 plus loginUrl and the web bundle shows the sign-in recovery.

### 2026-05-19 - Registration duplicate-email validation RLS follow-up
- Assessment: staging duplicate-email probe returned 500 because the public registration intent endpoint queried RLS-protected tenant/user tables with no current tenant; PostgreSQL tried to cast an empty tenant GUC to uuid.
- Task: added a scoped registration email lookup RLS policy, made tenant/user tenant policies use NULLIF(current_setting(..., true), '')::uuid, and wrapped the duplicate lookup in a transaction-local app.registration_email_lookup flag.
- Verification: focused Docker-backed registration tests passed 3/3, including duplicate email lookup against migrated PostgreSQL/RLS.
- Remaining risk: staging must apply the new RLS migration before the deployed API code can return 409 instead of the previous RLS 500.

## 2026-05-19 - registration Auth0 stale local session fix
- Assessment: Owner reproduced a wrong-account registration result: registering a new email could return to the app showing a previous app account. Staging data showed the new email had only a pending registration intent while the previous email had an active app user. OIDC registration-token validation already rejects mismatched Auth0 emails; the visible wrong-account state came from the previous local platform cookie surviving the failed/mismatched callback.
- Task: Changed /auth/login to revoke/sign out the local platform app session before issuing any Auth0 challenge, so registration or account-switch callbacks cannot fall back to a stale app cookie if Auth0 returns the wrong identity or the callback fails.
- Verification: RED AuthEndpoint regression proved registration login did not delete the app cookie. GREEN focused AuthEndpoint tests passed 3/3 after the fix, including app-cookie deletion before registration challenge and registration-token claim projection.

## 2026-05-19 - pending registration failed-auth recovery wording
- Assessment: Owner saw 'Verify email, then sign in' after signing out and trying to sign in again. Staging showed the attempted registration email still had a pending registration intent and no active app workspace/user, while the API rejected normal sign-in with no platform tenant membership. The UI was misclassifying pending-registration recovery as email verification or existing-workspace sign-in.
- Task: Updated /app?auth=failed so when a pending registration-token login URL exists, the recovery state says 'Finish workspace setup', explains that normal sign-in only works after membership exists, and labels the primary action 'Continue workspace setup'.
- Verification: Web production build passed. Focused Playwright recovery test passed 1/1 against the built preview server.

## 2026-05-19 - registration email verification grace flow

Assessment: Strict verified-email registration forced users through Auth0 signup, email verification, and a second workspace setup/login recovery path. That exposed implementation details as setup recovery, made stale Auth0/app sessions painful, and contradicted the intended normal signup path.

Task: Implemented the approved one-session registration grace flow. Registration-token OIDC callbacks can now create the workspace/session even when Auth0 reports `email_verified=false`; normal tenant sign-in remains fail-closed unless Auth0 currently reports `email_verified=true`. Added `external_auth_identity.email_verified_at` and `email_verification_grace_used_at`, resolver/session claim plumbing, `/auth/session.emailVerificationRequired`, and an authenticated in-app verify-email banner. Replaced broad pending workspace-setup recovery with recent structured registration-sign-in metadata and sign-in-specific recovery copy. Added tests for duplicate projection paths, normal-login rejection, registration grace persistence, structured pending metadata, stale metadata cleanup, and explicit false/no-banner UI behavior.

Verification: Fresh integrated backend verification passed 76/76 with `RUN_POSTGRES_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1` for focused auth/session/registration/mapping tests. Fresh web verification passed SvelteKit sync, production Vite build, and 8/8 focused Playwright tests for email-verification and pending-registration recovery. Review gates were run per task with spec and code-quality reviewers; blocking findings around unsafe overloads, missing migration metadata, incorrect resolver `EmailVerified`, legacy principal projection, normal-login fail-closed behavior, and stale pending recovery copy were fixed before merge.

Remaining risk: Existing app cookies minted before this change do not contain the new `email_verified` claim, so they will not show the banner until re-authentication. Pending-registration metadata validation is duplicated between `/register` and `/app`; if this state grows, extract a shared helper and consider stricter auth-origin/path allowlisting for the stored recovery URL.

## 2026-05-19 - last workspace sign-in recovery hotfix

Assessment: Owner reproduced a loop after successful registration grace: sign out from the newly created workspace, click home Sign in, and land on `/app?auth=failed` even after verifying email. Staging env showed the public `PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGIN_URL` lacked the new tenant and `PUBLIC_TENANT_ID` pointed at the seeded validation tenant. API logs confirmed the callback failed as no platform tenant membership was resolved, not as an email-verification failure.

Task: Added browser-side last-workspace tenant memory. Successful `/auth/session` now stores the authenticated tenant id in `localStorage`; home Sign in and `/app?auth=failed` existing-workspace recovery use that tenant id when building `/auth/login`. The default configured tenant remains the fallback for first-time/no-history browsers.

Verification: RED Playwright tests reproduced home Sign in staying `/auth/login` and failed recovery lacking the new tenant id. GREEN verification passed SvelteKit sync, production Vite build, and 10/10 focused Playwright tests for email verification, pending registration recovery, and last authenticated workspace sign-in.

Remaining risk: This is a browser-local single-last-workspace bridge, not a full multi-workspace account picker. Future multi-workspace support should replace this with server-backed workspace discovery or explicit workspace slug selection.

## 2026-05-19 - unverified sign-in recovery reason hotfix
Assessment: After sign-out from an unverified registration grace session, normal sign-in correctly failed because Auth0 still returned email_verified=false, but the browser saw generic workspace-access copy. Staging Docker logs showed OIDC email-unverified rejection followed by generic no-membership retries, meaning the backend rejection reason was being collapsed before the app recovery screen.
Task: Preserve the email_unverified reason from OIDC remote failure exception messages when auth properties are unavailable, and teach /app?auth=email_unverified to show verify-email-specific recovery copy instead of the generic workspace-access message.
Verification: Backend focused auth tests passed (6/6). Web production build passed by invoking local Vite through the absolute Node executable due local Windows npm PATH issues. Focused Playwright auth/registration recovery tests passed (11/11) against a direct preview server.
Remaining risk: If Auth0 keeps returning a stale email_verified=false claim after the user verifies email, the user may still need to sign out completely and sign in again to force a fresh Auth0 session claim.

## 2026-05-19 - verified-email recovery fresh Auth0 prompt
Assessment: Owner verified email after an unverified grace-session sign-out, clicked the recovery sign-in action, and still landed in failed auth. Staging API logs showed Auth0 continued returning `email_verified=false`; retries then fell into generic no-membership failures. The recovery link reused the existing Auth0 SSO session instead of forcing a fresh authorization prompt.
Task: Updated the `/app?auth=email_unverified` recovery CTA so `Sign in after verifying email` appends `prompt=login` to the workspace login URL while preserving the stored last-workspace tenant target and leaving other failed-auth recovery actions unchanged.
Verification: RED Playwright regression first failed with `/auth/login?tenantId=...` missing `prompt=login`. GREEN web production build passed via direct Node/Vite invocation. Focused Playwright regression passed 1/1, then the broader auth/registration recovery set passed 11/11.
Remaining risk: This forces a fresh Auth0 prompt but still depends on Auth0 returning an updated `email_verified=true` claim after the user has actually completed email verification.

## 2026-05-19 - verified-email recovery account hint
Assessment: Owner still landed on generic workspace-access failure after verifying email and clicking the recovery sign-in action. Staging logs showed the first retry could still be email-unverified, but the post-verification failure became `no platform tenant membership was resolved`. Recent staging workspaces existed with grace-used identities and no verified identity update, indicating the fresh Auth0 prompt was not reliably selecting the same workspace email.
Task: Remember the authenticated workspace email in browser storage alongside the last tenant id, add `login_hint` support to generated login URLs, and include that hint on home sign-in and `/app?auth=email_unverified` recovery so Auth0 targets the account that owns the remembered workspace.
Verification: RED `session-headers` unit tests failed for missing `login_hint` and missing email storage helpers. GREEN unit tests passed 9/9. Web production build passed via direct Node/Vite invocation. Focused Playwright account-targeting tests passed 3/3, and broader auth/registration recovery tests passed 11/11.
Remaining risk: `login_hint` guides Auth0 account selection but cannot prove the user typed the hinted account password. Wrong-account sign-in can still fail closed, but the normal recovery path now targets the correct workspace email.

## 2026-05-19 - registration stores workspace account before redirect
Assessment: Owner still hit generic workspace-access failure after verifying email. The previous login_hint fix depended on browser storage already containing the workspace email, but the registration success path redirected to `/app` without persisting the `CreateRegistrationWorkspaceResponse` tenant/email first. If the later app session did not populate the email before sign-out, the recovery login URL could still lack the correct account hint.
Task: Store the created workspace tenant id and email in localStorage immediately after `/registration/workspaces` succeeds and before redirecting to the app. Added Playwright coverage for registration workspace creation persisting both values.
Verification: RED Playwright regression timed out because registration did not store tenant/email. GREEN web production build passed, focused registration-storage test passed 1/1, and broader auth/registration recovery set passed 12/12.
Remaining risk: For browser state created before this hotfix, a fresh registration attempt or one successful app session after this deploy is needed to populate the remembered workspace email.

## 2026-05-19 - temporary Auth0 workspace login diagnostics
Assessment: Owner still hit failed-auth loops after registration, email verification, sign-out, and normal sign-in. Existing staging logs only showed generic no-membership resolution failures, which was not enough to distinguish wrong Auth0 account selection, missing user persistence, missing role membership, disabled/mismatched external binding, or unverified provider claims.
Task: Added temporary safe OIDC resolver diagnostics behind the `[AUTH-DIAG-20260519]` log prefix. The resolver logs only short hashes for tenant/email/provider subject plus the exact resolution outcome; it does not log raw email, tenant id, provider subject, tokens, or session identifiers.
Verification: Pending API build/deploy and owner retry against staging.
Remaining risk: Diagnostics are intentionally temporary and should be removed or downgraded after the auth loop root cause is confirmed.

## 2026-05-19 - workspace tenant carried through registration/logout sign-in recovery
Assessment: Owner retried with `majeric.martin+334@gmail.com`. Temporary API diagnostics showed Auth0 returned that email with `emailVerified=True`, but normal sign-in resolved against tenant hash `bd7662a5eeb41614`, which matches the seeded `PUBLIC_TENANT_ID=11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111`, while the created `+334` workspace tenant hashes to `6c56e4cf8a91d4db`. The database confirmed the `+334` user, external binding, and tenant role membership exist in the newly created workspace. The failure was therefore a browser/web sign-in target problem, not missing persistence or membership.
Task: Changed registration to redirect to `/app?tenantId=<created workspace>`, app sign-out to return to `/?tenantId=<current workspace>`, and home/app sign-in URL generation to prefer a tenant id from the current URL before localStorage or the old public default. Added tenant-id normalization reuse in the session-header helper and Playwright coverage for registration redirect, sign-out return URL, home sign-in, and failed-auth recovery.
Verification: Web production Vite build passed through direct Node invocation; existing large chunk warning only. Focused Playwright coverage passed 3/3 by reusing a direct preview server because the local Playwright webServer command still calls bare `npm`, which this Windows shell does not expose.
Remaining risk: `PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGIN_URL` remains a global fallback and is still a poor fit for self-serve multi-workspace sign-in. It should be replaced by a generated auth route helper or server-provided workspace login URL before real multi-workspace/account-picker work.

## 2026-05-19 - remove seeded public tenant/auth URL fallback from web sign-in
Assessment: Owner inspected the staging home page source after sign-out and saw `PUBLIC_TENANT_ID`, `PUBLIC_DEV_TENANT_ID`, `PUBLIC_DEV_USER_ID`, and full `PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGIN_URL`/`PUBLIC_AUTH_LOGOUT_URL` values in the client env. This confirmed the remaining design problem: the browser could still discover and reuse a seeded/default workspace context even though self-serve registration needs explicit workspace context.
Task: Changed the web auth URL helper so normal login no longer falls back to `PUBLIC_TENANT_ID`; it only uses an explicit tenant id from URL/storage/session. Login/logout endpoints are derived from `PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` when no explicit auth URL override is supplied. Register sign-in now uses remembered workspace context when available. Staging compose no longer injects public seeded tenant/user ids or full public auth URLs into the web container.
Verification: Session-header unit tests passed 10/10. Web production Vite build passed through direct Node invocation; existing large chunk warning only. Focused Playwright coverage for home sign-in after sign-out, registration workspace memory, and failed-auth recovery passed 3/3 against a direct preview server.
Remaining risk: First-time "sign in" without a known workspace still has no product-grade workspace discovery/account picker. That is a separate design slice; this change prevents silent fallback to the seeded tenant.

## 2026-05-19 - existing workspace sign-in lookup
Assessment: After removing the seeded public tenant fallback, bare `/auth/login` correctly fails with `400 Invalid login context`. The web still exposed generic Sign in links when no tenant context was remembered, so first-time or signed-out existing users could hit the backend without `tenantId`.
Task: Added `POST /registration/workspace-sign-in` for beta existing-workspace lookup by email. It validates email and return URL, uses the existing registration email lookup RLS guard, finds the active workspace tenant for the email, and returns a tenant-scoped `/auth/login` URL with `prompt=login` and `login_hint`. The home page now routes generic Sign in to `/register?mode=signin`; the register page has an email-only sign-in mode that remembers the returned tenant before redirecting to Auth0. Known-workspace sign-in still goes directly to Auth0 with tenant context.
Verification: API release build passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors. Web registration/session-header unit tests passed 14/14. Web production build passed; existing large chunk warning only. Focused Playwright auth/registration tests passed 5/5 for generic sign-in lookup, existing workspace lookup, last-workspace sign-in, registration workspace memory, and failed-auth recovery.
Remaining risk: This is still beta one-email-one-workspace lookup, not a full multi-workspace picker. If one email later belongs to multiple workspaces, this endpoint needs to return choices instead of first tenant.

## 2026-05-19 - local auth cleanup before next deploy
Assessment: Owner confirmed staging sign-in finally works, then selected a local-only cleanup pass before any next deployment. The temporary Auth0 resolver diagnostics are no longer needed after identifying the real issue as missing explicit workspace context. The remaining product gap is that first-time existing users were routed through `/register?mode=signin`, which made registration and sign-in feel like the same flow.

Task: Remove the temporary `[AUTH-DIAG-20260519]` OIDC resolver diagnostics, add a dedicated `/signin` workspace email lookup page, route generic home sign-in there when no workspace is remembered, keep `/register` focused on create-account/create-workspace flow, and document the Auth0/platform split of responsibilities in `20-architecture/auth0-platform-auth-model.md`.

Verification: API release build passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors. Web registration/session-header unit tests passed 14/14. Web production build passed; existing large chunk warning only. Focused Playwright auth/registration coverage passed 5/5 against a local production preview. `rg` confirmed the temporary `[AUTH-DIAG-20260519]` resolver diagnostics are gone. Not deployed.

Remaining risk: `/signin` is still the beta one-email-one-workspace lookup, not a multi-workspace picker. Future multi-workspace support must replace the lookup response with explicit workspace choices.

## 2026-05-19 - sign-in chrome and email-verification recovery fix
Assessment: Owner found two staging defects after the dedicated `/signin` deploy. `/signin` still showed the setup sidebar/header because `AppShell` treated it as a tenant setup route instead of a public entry route. `/app?auth=email_unverified` could send users to `/register` when no remembered tenant login target existed because app auth recovery still used registration as the no-context fallback.

Task: Mark `/signin` as a public entry route in `AppShell`, so it uses the same chrome-free treatment as `/` and `/register`. Change no-context app auth recovery to `/signin` instead of `/register`, keep tenant-context email verification recovery as direct `/auth/login` with `prompt=login`, and add Playwright regressions for both paths.

Verification: Web production build passed; existing large chunk warning only. Focused Playwright auth/registration recovery coverage passed 8/8 locally. VPS staging rebuilt/recreated from `988f003`; the first redeploy proof hit a transient web root `502` during warm-up, then follow-up checks returned API `/health/ready` 200, web `/` 200, and web `/signin` 200. `/signin` HTML no longer contained setup-stage/sidebar text. Clean VPS release evidence passed at `/tmp/auth-recovery-vps-release-20260519`: public smoke passed, backup/restore passed, authenticated remote smoke skipped.

Deployment update:
- Commit pushed to `origin/main` and `origin/staging`: `988f003 fix(auth): clean sign-in recovery chrome`.
- VPS staging checkout fast-forwarded to `988f003`.
- Running web image after rebuild: `sha256:79662ff785d42a324ebb309f1d3bd695dae40bb2c311608279fc8e2c29660995`.
- Running API image after rebuild: `sha256:f9adec17dab2bd7b5e3ed606a06f1e7013c34196eb718a29a691c697c09782e7`.

## 2026-05-19 - deferred auth boundary questions
Assessment: After the `/signin` cleanup, the remaining auth cleanup items are intentionally future-facing: renaming the beta existing-workspace lookup route and replacing one-email-one-workspace lookup with a proper workspace picker if multi-workspace accounts become real. Neither blocks the current beta flow, but both should stay visible so they are not mistaken for forgotten bugs.

Task: Added Q-056 for the auth/account API route boundary and Q-057 for future multi-workspace account picker design.

Verification: Documentation-only update; no runtime verification required yet.

Remaining risk: Q-056 and Q-057 remain open. Current beta remains one active workspace membership per normalized email.

## 2026-05-19 - D348 auth onboarding product hardening

Assessment: The backend Auth0/workspace flow was stable enough for beta testing, but the product still did not clearly explain the post-signup path, the Auth0/platform responsibility split, tenant member first sign-in, or directory hierarchy semantics. The remaining route rename and multi-workspace picker work is future-facing rather than required for the current one-email-one-workspace beta.

Task: Added `docs/v2/40-ops/staging-auth-flow-checklist.md` and `docs/plans/2026-05-19-auth-onboarding-hardening.md`. Added a first-run setup runway to `/app`, team-member onboarding steps and pending-provider-link copy to `/app/team`, and directory setup-order plus hierarchy-versus-app-role guidance to `/app/directory`. Added focused Playwright checks for the new first-run, team, and directory guidance while keeping the recent sign-in and email-verification recovery checks in the same focused run. Added assessment `docs/v2/80-agent-handoff/assessments/D348-AUTH-ONBOARDING-PRODUCT-HARDENING-ASSESSMENT-2026-05-19.md`.

Verification: Web production build passed through direct Node invocation. Focused Playwright run passed 8/8 against local production preview: generic home sign-in lookup, registration email-verification recovery, unverified workspace sign-in recovery, no-remembered-workspace verification recovery, existing workspace email lookup, first-run workspace runway, team member first sign-in guidance, and directory hierarchy setup order.

Remaining risk: Not deployed. Current beta still assumes one active workspace per normalized email. Q-056 and Q-057 remain open. Staging owner retest is still required before treating this browser flow as proven in the hosted environment.
## 2026-05-19 - selected-series visible metadata cleanup

Assessment: Owner testing showed selected-study pages still exposed raw series ids, duplicate selected-series navigation inside the body, and ISO/nanosecond timestamps before useful setup content. This made the app feel like an internal proof surface instead of a product workflow.

Task: Removed duplicated in-body selected-series navigation from the hub and selected-series surfaces, removed normal campaign-series id rows from selected-series reference sections, moved remaining hub technical details behind a collapsed section, removed tenant/user id rows from the session profile view, and formatted selected-series/settings/export timestamps with Croatian-style day/month/year 24-hour display.

Verification: Not run separately by request constraints; deployment build will be the syntax gate if deployed.
## 2026-05-19 - setup workflow wizard presentation

Assessment: Owner testing showed the setup page still read like a long internal checklist: template-version wording was unclear, the current setup task copy was too technical, and the user had to scroll through too much material instead of working one setup step at a time.

Task: Reframed setup workflow labels around instrument templates, draft campaigns, and launch checks. Added an active-step setup wizard shell with a persistent compact setup path, previous/next controls, clickable available/done steps, automatic advance after successful setup actions, and hidden campaign/audience detail sections unless the active step is campaign or launch readiness. Removed action result id lines from normal setup feedback.

Verification: Not run separately by request constraints; deployment build will be the syntax gate if deployed.
